revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]新しいページ: ''''太秦古墳群'''（うずまさこふんぐん）は[[大阪府]][[寝屋川市]]東部の丘陵上に所在する古墳群。 [[古墳時代]]中期か...'
revision.text:
'''太秦古墳群'''（うずまさこふんぐん）は[[大阪府]][[寝屋川市]]東部の丘陵上に所在する古墳群。 [[古墳時代]]中期から後期にかけての[[群集墳]]ではないかと考えられている。 == 概要== 寝屋川市東部の太秦高塚町、国守町とその周辺の丘陵には[[円墳]]と考えられる太秦高塚古墳の存在が以前から知られており、それ以外にも各所から[[埴輪]]や[[須恵器]]が出土しており、かつてはかなりの広がりのある古墳群が存在したようである。これらを総称して太秦古墳群と呼ばれるようになっていた。京都と大阪を結ぶ、第二京阪国道の建設に伴って2001年から2003年にかけて大阪府文化財センターが道路予定地内の事前調査を行い、古墳時代中期から古墳時代後期にかけての墳丘を削平された小規模な古墳25基を発掘している。これらの古墳は尾根上で検出されたことから太秦古墳群の尾支群と呼ばれることになった。また同じく2001年には寝屋川市により太秦高塚古墳が[[史跡]]整備のため発掘調査されており、その全容がほぼ明らかにされている。 ===尾支群の調査=== 第二京阪道路建設による事前調査で明らかにされたもので、尾根上に古墳の周溝と考えられる遺構が多数確認された。調査前は、ほとんど削られており、主体部が明らかにされたのは1基（３号墳）のみで木棺直葬であった。古墳の規模は1辺または直径が4－14メートルの範囲のものであった。遺物は各古墳の周溝から須恵器や[[土師器]]が出土しているが埴輪が出土したのは2基（8・17号墳）のみで[[円筒埴輪]]、朝顔形埴輪が出土している。これらの遺物から尾支群は5世紀中葉から6世紀前葉に築造されたと考えられる。 ===太秦高塚古墳=== 尾支群の北西800メートルに位置する古墳で太秦古墳群では墳丘が完全に残る唯一の古墳である。2001年に史跡整備に伴う発掘調査が実施された。墳丘は北西部に造り出しを有する直径37メートルを測る円墳であり、2段に築かれ、[[葺石]]はなかった。墳丘のテラスの部分には円筒埴輪列、造り出し部でも円筒埴輪列が確認されており、加えて人物、家、盾、鶏、水鳥などの形象埴輪が出土した。墳頂部では埋葬主体が確認され、短甲、鏃、斧、馬具などの副葬品が出土している。埴輪などから5世紀後半の築造と考えられる。同じ太秦古墳でも、この高塚古墳と尾支群とでは墳丘規模、出土遺物などで大きな格差があり、当時の階層を反映していると考えられる。群集墳を構成する集団のリーダーの墓が太秦高塚古墳、集団の構成員の墓が尾支群のような群小古墳ではないかと考えられる。
現在、太秦高塚古墳は寝屋川市指定史跡に指定され、「太秦高塚古墳公園」内に整備保存されている。
==参考文献== 市本芳三「太秦古墳群」『シンポジウム 大阪の初期古墳を考える』1-3頁 大阪府立弥生文化博物館 2005年
==連項目== [[長原古墳群]] ==関連項目== *[http://www.city.neyagawa.osaka.jp/index/soshiki/bunka/utkofun01.html] 太秦高塚古墳史跡整備事業 （寝屋川市役所）
{{デフォルトソート:うずまさこふんぐん}} * [[category:古墳]] * [[category:大阪府の古墳]] * [[category:寝屋川市]] * [[category:河内国]]

'''太秦古墳群'''（うずまさこふんぐん）は[[大阪府]][[寝屋川市]]東部の丘陵上に所在する[[古墳群]]。 [[古墳時代]]中期から後期にかけての[[群集墳]]ではないかと考えられている。
== 概要== 寝屋川市東部の太秦高塚町、国守町とその周辺の丘陵には[[円墳]]と考えられる太秦高塚古墳の存在が以前から知られており、それ以外にも各所から[[埴輪]]や[[須恵器]]が出土しており、かつてはかなりの広がりのある古墳群が存在したようである。これらを総称して太秦古墳群と呼ばれるようになっていた。京都と大阪を結ぶ、第二京阪国道の建設に伴って2001年から2003年にかけて大阪府文化財センターが道路予定地内の事前調査を行い、古墳時代中期から古墳時代後期にかけての墳丘を削平された小規模な古墳25基を発掘している。これらの古墳は尾根上で検出されたことから太秦古墳群の尾支群と呼ばれることになった。また同じく2001年には寝屋川市により太秦高塚古墳が[[史跡]]整備のため発掘調査されており、その全容がほぼ明らかにされている。 ===尾支群の調査=== 第二京阪道路建設による事前調査で明らかにされたもので、尾根上に古墳の周溝と考えられる遺構が多数確認された。調査前は、ほとんど削られており、主体部が明らかにされたのは1基（３号墳）のみで木棺直葬であった。古墳の規模は1辺または直径が4－14メートルの範囲のものであった。遺物は各古墳の周溝から須恵器や[[土師器]]が出土しているが埴輪が出土したのは2基（8・17号墳）のみで[[円筒埴輪]]、朝顔形埴輪が出土している。これらの遺物から尾支群は5世紀中葉から6世紀前葉に築造されたと考えられる。 ===太秦高塚古墳=== 尾支群の北西800メートルに位置する古墳で太秦古墳群では墳丘が完全に残る唯一の古墳である。2001年に史跡整備に伴う発掘調査が実施された。墳丘は北西部に造り出しを有する直径37メートルを測る円墳であり、2段に築かれ、[[葺石]]はなかった。墳丘のテラスの部分には円筒埴輪列、造り出し部でも円筒埴輪列が確認されており、加えて人物、家、盾、鶏、水鳥などの形象埴輪が出土した。墳頂部では埋葬主体が確認され、短甲、鏃、斧、馬具などの副葬品が出土している。埴輪などから5世紀後半の築造と考えられる。同じ太秦古墳でも、この高塚古墳と尾支群とでは墳丘規模、出土遺物などで大きな格差があり、当時の階層を反映していると考えられる。群集墳を構成する集団のリーダーの墓が太秦高塚古墳、集団の構成員の墓が尾支群のような群小古墳ではないかと考えられる。
現在、太秦高塚古墳は寝屋川市指定史跡に指定され、「太秦高塚古墳公園」内に整備保存されている。
==参考文献== 市本芳三「太秦古墳群」『シンポジウム 大阪の初期古墳を考える』1-3頁 大阪府立弥生文化博物館 2005年
==連項目== [[長原古墳群]]
==関連項目== *[http://www.city.neyagawa.osaka.jp/index/soshiki/bunka/utkofun01.html 太秦高塚古墳史跡整備事業]（寝屋川市役所）
{{デフォルトソート:うすまさこふんくん}} [[category:大阪府の古墳]] [[category:寝屋川市]] [[category:河内国]]

==関連項目== *[http://www.city.neyagawa.osaka.jp/index/soshiki/bunka/utkofun01.html 太秦高塚古墳史跡整備事業]（寝屋川市役所）
{{デフォルトソート:うすまさこふんくん}} [[category:大阪府の古墳]] [[category:寝屋川市]] [[category:河内国]]

'''太秦古墳群'''（うずまさこふんぐん）は[[大阪府]][[寝屋川市]]東部の丘陵上に所在する[[古墳群]]。 [[古墳時代]]中期から後期にかけての[[群集墳]]ではないかと考えられている。
== 概要== 寝屋川市東部の太秦高塚町、国守町とその周辺の丘陵には[[円墳]]と考えられる太秦高塚古墳の存在が以前から知られており、それ以外にも各所から[[埴輪]]や[[須恵器]]が出土しており、かつては、かなりの広がりのある古墳群が存在したようである。これらを総称して太秦古墳群と呼ぶようになっている。京都と大阪を結ぶ、[[第二京阪道路]]の建設に伴って2001年から2003年にかけて大阪府文化財センターが道路予定地内の事前調査を行い、古墳時代中期から後期にかけての[[墳丘]]を削平された小規模な古墳25基を発掘している。これらの古墳は尾根上で検出されたことから太秦古墳群の尾支群と呼ばれることになった。また同じく2001年には寝屋川市により太秦高塚古墳が[[史跡]]整備のため発掘調査されており、その全容がほぼ明らかにされている。
===尾支群の調査=== 第二京阪道路建設による事前調査で明らかにされたもので、尾根上に古墳の周溝と考えられる遺構が多数確認された。調査前は、ほとんど削られており、主体部が明らかにされたのは1基（3号墳）のみで木棺直葬であった。古墳の規模は1辺または直径が4－14メートルの範囲のものであった。遺物は各古墳の周溝から須恵器や[[土師器]]が出土しているが埴輪が出土したのは2基（8・17号墳）のみで[[円筒埴輪]]、朝顔形埴輪が出土している。これらの遺物から尾支群は5世紀中葉から6世紀前葉に築造されたと考えられる<ref>市本(2005)p1.</ref>。
===太秦高塚古墳=== 尾支群の北西800メートルに位置する古墳で太秦古墳群では墳丘が完全に残る唯一の古墳である。2001年に史跡整備に伴う発掘調査が実施された。墳丘は北西部に造り出しを有する直径37メートルを測る円墳であり、2段に築かれ、[[葺石]]はなかった。墳丘のテラスの部分には円筒埴輪列、造り出し部でも円筒埴輪列が確認されており、加えて人物、家、盾、鶏、水鳥などの[[形象埴輪]]が出土した。墳頂部では埋葬主体が確認され、短甲、鏃、斧、馬具などの[[副葬品]]が出土している。埴輪などから5世紀後半の築造と考えられる。同じ太秦古墳でも、この高塚古墳と尾支群とでは墳丘規模、出土遺物などで大きな格差があり、当時の階層を反映していると考えられる。群集墳を構成する集団のリーダーの墓が太秦高塚古墳、集団の構成員の墓が尾支群のような群小古墳ではないかと考えられる。
現在、太秦高塚古墳は寝屋川市指定史跡に指定され、「太秦高塚古墳公園」内に整備保存されている。
==参考文献== *市本芳三「太秦古墳群」『シンポジウム 大阪の初期古墳を考える』1-3頁 大阪府立弥生文化博物館 2006年 *『太秦古墳群 発掘調査成果 大阪の初期群集墳を考える』 編集 財団法人大阪文化財センター 2006年

'''太秦古墳群'''（うずまさこふんぐん）は、[[大阪府]][[寝屋川市]]東部の丘陵上に所在する[[古墳群]]。[[古墳時代]]中期から後期にかけての[[群集墳]]ではないかと考えられている。
== 概要== 寝屋川市東部の太秦高塚町、国守町とその周辺の丘陵には、[[円墳]]と考えられる太秦高塚古墳の存在が以前から知られていた。それ以外にも各所から[[埴輪]]や[[須恵器]]が出土しており、付近には小金塚、向ヒ塚、廻し塚、モロ塚、上ノ山、墓ノ山、松谷山、段谷山など古墳の存在を窺わせる「塚」や「山」のつく小字名が知られ、かつては、かなりの広がりのある古墳群が存在したようである。1962年には太秦熱田神社の裏山から牡鹿の頭部と考えられる埴輪が出土したり、1965年には太秦高塚古墳の南約300メートルで木棺直葬と考えられる主体部が発見され、鉄鏃、直刀などが出土している。これ以外にも三環鈴、金環、銅鏃、子持勾玉2、勾玉3、紡錘車2などが付近にあった古墳の出土遺物として知られている。これらを総称して太秦古墳群と呼ばれている<ref>西田(1994)p85.</ref>。京都と大阪を結ぶ、[[第二京阪道路]]の建設に伴って2001年から2003年にかけて大阪府文化財センターが道路予定地内の事前調査を行い、古墳時代中期から後期にかけての[[墳丘]]を削平された小規模な古墳25基を発掘している。これらの古墳は尾根上で検出されたことから太秦古墳群の尾支群と呼ばれることになった。また同じく2001年には、寝屋川市により太秦高塚古墳が[[史跡]]整備のため発掘調査されており、その全容がほぼ明らかにされている。
===尾支群の調査=== [[第二京阪道路]]建設による事前調査で明らかにされたもので、尾根上に古墳の周溝と考えられる遺構が多数確認された。調査前は、ほとんど削られており、主体部が明らかにされたのは1基（3号墳）のみで木棺直葬であった。古墳の規模は1辺または直径が4－14メートルの範囲のものであった。遺物は各古墳の周溝から須恵器や[[土師器]]が出土しているが埴輪が出土したのは2基（8・17号墳）のみで[[円筒埴輪]]、朝顔形埴輪が出土している。これらの遺物から尾支群は5世紀中葉から6世紀前葉に築造されたと考えられる<ref>市本(2005)p1.</ref>。
===太秦高塚古墳=== 尾支群の北西800メートルに位置する古墳で、太秦古墳群では墳丘が完全に残る唯一の古墳である。2001年に史跡整備に伴う発掘調査が実施された。墳丘は北西部に造り出しを有する直径37メートルを測る円墳であり、2段に築かれ、[[葺石]]はなかった。墳丘のテラスの部分には円筒埴輪列、造り出し部でも円筒埴輪列が確認されており、加えて人物、家、盾、鶏、水鳥などの[[形象埴輪]]が出土した。墳頂部では埋葬主体が確認され、短甲、鏃、斧、馬具などの[[副葬品]]が出土している。埴輪などから5世紀後半の築造と考えられる。同じ太秦古墳でも、この高塚古墳と尾支群とでは墳丘規模、出土遺物などで大きな格差があり、当時の階層を反映していると考えられる。群集墳を構成する集団のリーダーの墓が太秦高塚古墳、集団の構成員の墓が尾支群のような群小古墳ではないかと考えられる。
現在、太秦高塚古墳は寝屋川市指定史跡に指定され、「太秦高塚古墳公園」内に整備保存されている。
==参考文献== *西太敏秀「茨田の古墳」『楽しい古墳めぐり』松籟社 84-86頁 *市本芳三「太秦古墳群」『シンポジウム 大阪の初期古墳を考える』1-3頁 大阪府立弥生文化博物館 2006年 *『太秦古墳群 発掘調査成果 大阪の初期群集墳を考える』 編集 財団法人大阪文化財センター 2006年
==脚注== {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}}
==関連項目== *[[長原古墳群]]
==外部リンク== *[http://www.city.neyagawa.osaka.jp/index/soshiki/bunka/utkofun01.html 太秦高塚古墳史跡整備事業]（寝屋川市役所）
{{デフォルトソート:うすまさこふんくん}} [[category:大阪府の古墳]] [[category:寝屋川市の歴史]] [[category:河内国]]

revision.comment: /* 外部リンク */ [[Category:群集墳]]追加。
revision.text:
'''太秦古墳群'''（うずまさこふんぐん）は、[[大阪府]][[寝屋川市]]東部の丘陵上に所在する[[古墳群]]。[[古墳時代]]中期から後期にかけての[[群集墳]]ではないかと考えられている。
== 概要== 寝屋川市東部の太秦高塚町、国守町とその周辺の丘陵には、[[円墳]]と考えられる太秦高塚古墳の存在が以前から知られていた。それ以外にも各所から[[埴輪]]や[[須恵器]]が出土しており、付近には小金塚、向ヒ塚、廻し塚、モロ塚、上ノ山、墓ノ山、松谷山、段谷山など古墳の存在を窺わせる「塚」や「山」のつく小字名が知られ、かつては、かなりの広がりのある古墳群が存在したようである。1962年には太秦熱田神社の裏山から牡鹿の頭部と考えられる埴輪が出土したり、1965年には太秦高塚古墳の南約300メートルで木棺直葬と考えられる主体部が発見され、鉄鏃、直刀などが出土している。これ以外にも三環鈴、金環、銅鏃、子持勾玉2、勾玉3、紡錘車2などが付近にあった古墳の出土遺物として知られている。これらを総称して太秦古墳群と呼ばれている<ref>西田(1994)p85.</ref>。京都と大阪を結ぶ、[[第二京阪道路]]の建設に伴って2001年から2003年にかけて大阪府文化財センターが道路予定地内の事前調査を行い、古墳時代中期から後期にかけての[[墳丘]]を削平された小規模な古墳25基を発掘している。これらの古墳は尾根上で検出されたことから太秦古墳群の尾支群と呼ばれることになった。また同じく2001年には、寝屋川市により太秦高塚古墳が[[史跡]]整備のため発掘調査されており、その全容がほぼ明らかにされている。
===尾支群の調査=== [[第二京阪道路]]建設による事前調査で明らかにされたもので、尾根上に古墳の周溝と考えられる遺構が多数確認された。調査前は、ほとんど削られており、主体部が明らかにされたのは1基（3号墳）のみで木棺直葬であった。古墳の規模は1辺または直径が4－14メートルの範囲のものであった。遺物は各古墳の周溝から須恵器や[[土師器]]が出土しているが埴輪が出土したのは2基（8・17号墳）のみで[[円筒埴輪]]、朝顔形埴輪が出土している。これらの遺物から尾支群は5世紀中葉から6世紀前葉に築造されたと考えられる<ref>市本(2005)p1.</ref>。
===太秦高塚古墳=== [[File:UzumasaTakatsuka kofun.JPG|thumb|太秦高塚古墳]] 尾支群の北西800メートルに位置する古墳で、太秦古墳群では墳丘が完全に残る唯一の古墳である。2001年に史跡整備に伴う発掘調査が実施された。墳丘は北西部に[[造り出し]]を有する直径37メートルを測る円墳であり、2段に築かれ、[[葺石]]はなかった。墳丘のテラスの部分には円筒埴輪列、造り出し部でも円筒埴輪列が確認されており、加えて人物、家、盾、鶏、水鳥などの[[形象埴輪]]が出土した。墳頂部では埋葬主体が確認され、短甲、鏃、斧、馬具などの[[副葬品]]が出土している。埴輪などから5世紀後半の築造と考えられる。同じ太秦古墳でも、この高塚古墳と尾支群とでは墳丘規模、出土遺物などで大きな格差があり、当時の階層を反映していると考えられる。群集墳を構成する集団のリーダーの墓が太秦高塚古墳、集団の構成員の墓が尾支群のような群小古墳ではないかと考えられる。
現在、太秦高塚古墳は寝屋川市指定史跡に指定され、「太秦高塚古墳公園」内に整備保存されている。
==参考文献== *西太敏秀「茨田の古墳」『楽しい古墳めぐり』松籟社 84-86頁 *市本芳三「太秦古墳群」『シンポジウム 大阪の初期古墳を考える』1-3頁 大阪府立弥生文化博物館 2006年 *『太秦古墳群 発掘調査成果 大阪の初期群集墳を考える』 編集 財団法人大阪文化財センター 2006年
==脚注== {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}}

==外部リンク== *[http://www.city.neyagawa.osaka.jp/organization_list/kyoiku_shakaikyoiku/bunkasport/bunkazai/1378005964199.html 太秦高塚古墳史跡整備事業]（寝屋川市役所）

== 文化財 == === 寝屋川市指定文化財 === * 有形文化財 ** 太秦古墳群出土鹿形埴輪（考古資料） - 寝屋川市立埋蔵文化財資料館保管。2000年（平成12年）11月3日指定<ref name="一覧">[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html 大阪府内指定文化財一覧表]（大阪府ホームページ、平成30年11月21日更新版）の寝屋川市ファイルより。</ref>。 * 史跡 ** 太秦高塚古墳 - 1997年（平成9年）11月3日指定<ref name="一覧"/>。

== 概要== [[File:Uzumasa Kofun Group-18, funkyu.jpg|thumb|220px|right|18号墳 再現墳丘]] 寝屋川市東部の太秦高塚町、国守町とその周辺の丘陵には、[[円墳]]と考えられる太秦高塚古墳の存在が以前から知られていた。それ以外にも各所から[[埴輪]]や[[須恵器]]が出土しており、付近には小金塚、向ヒ塚、廻し塚、モロ塚、上ノ山、墓ノ山、松谷山、段谷山など古墳の存在を窺わせる「塚」や「山」のつく小字名が知られ、かつては、かなりの広がりのある古墳群が存在したようである。1962年には太秦熱田神社の裏山から牡鹿の頭部と考えられる埴輪が出土したり、1965年には太秦高塚古墳の南約300メートルで木棺直葬と考えられる主体部が発見され、鉄鏃、直刀などが出土している。これ以外にも三環鈴、金環、銅鏃、子持勾玉2、勾玉3、紡錘車2などが付近にあった古墳の出土遺物として知られている。これらを総称して太秦古墳群と呼ばれている<ref>西田(1994)p85.</ref>。京都と大阪を結ぶ、[[第二京阪道路]]の建設に伴って2001年から2003年にかけて大阪府文化財センターが道路予定地内の事前調査を行い、古墳時代中期から後期にかけての[[墳丘]]を削平された小規模な古墳25基を発掘している。これらの古墳は尾根上で検出されたことから太秦古墳群の尾支群と呼ばれることになった。また同じく2001年には、寝屋川市により太秦高塚古墳が[[史跡]]整備のため発掘調査されており、その全容がほぼ明らかにされている。
===尾支群の調査=== [[第二京阪道路]]建設による事前調査で明らかにされたもので、尾根上に古墳の周溝と考えられる遺構が多数確認された。調査前は、ほとんど削られており、主体部が明らかにされたのは1基（3号墳）のみで木棺直葬であった。古墳の規模は1辺または直径が4－14メートルの範囲のものであった。遺物は各古墳の周溝から須恵器や[[土師器]]が出土しているが埴輪が出土したのは2基（8・17号墳）のみで[[円筒埴輪]]、朝顔形埴輪が出土している。これらの遺物から尾支群は5世紀中葉から6世紀前葉に築造されたと考えられる<ref>市本(2005)p1.</ref>。
===太秦高塚古墳=== [[File:UzumasaTakatsuka kofun.JPG|thumb|太秦高塚古墳]] 尾支群の北西800メートルに位置する古墳で、太秦古墳群では墳丘が完全に残る唯一の古墳である。2001年に史跡整備に伴う発掘調査が実施された。墳丘は北西部に[[造り出し]]を有する直径37メートルを測る円墳であり、2段に築かれ、[[葺石]]はなかった。墳丘のテラスの部分には円筒埴輪列、造り出し部でも円筒埴輪列が確認されており、加えて人物、家、盾、鶏、水鳥などの[[形象埴輪]]が出土した。墳頂部では埋葬主体が確認され、短甲、鏃、斧、馬具などの[[副葬品]]が出土している。埴輪などから5世紀後半の築造と考えられる。同じ太秦古墳でも、この高塚古墳と尾支群とでは墳丘規模、出土遺物などで大きな格差があり、当時の階層を反映していると考えられる。群集墳を構成する集団のリーダーの墓が太秦高塚古墳、集団の構成員の墓が尾支群のような群小古墳ではないかと考えられる。
現在、太秦高塚古墳は寝屋川市指定史跡に指定され、「太秦高塚古墳公園」内に整備保存されている。
== 文化財 == === 寝屋川市指定文化財 === * 有形文化財 ** 太秦古墳群出土鹿形埴輪（考古資料） - 寝屋川市立埋蔵文化財資料館保管。2000年（平成12年）11月3日指定<ref name="一覧">[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html 大阪府内指定文化財一覧表]（大阪府ホームページ、平成30年11月21日更新版）の寝屋川市ファイルより。</ref>。 * 史跡 ** 太秦高塚古墳 - 1997年（平成9年）11月3日指定<ref name="一覧"/>。
==脚注== {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
==参考文献== *西太敏秀「茨田の古墳」『楽しい古墳めぐり』松籟社 84-86頁 *市本芳三「太秦古墳群」『シンポジウム 大阪の初期古墳を考える』1-3頁 大阪府立弥生文化博物館 2006年 *『太秦古墳群 発掘調査成果 大阪の初期群集墳を考える』 編集 財団法人大阪文化財センター 2006年
==関連項目== *[[長原古墳群]]
==外部リンク== {{Commonscat|Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun|太秦高塚古墳}} *[https://www.city.neyagawa.osaka.jp/organization_list/kyoiku_shakaikyoiku/bunkasport/bunkazai/namesagasu/1378193397977.html 太秦古墳群]、[https://www.city.neyagawa.osaka.jp/Recommend/bunkazai/neyagawashishiteibunkazai/1382432802559.html 太秦高塚古墳] - 寝屋川市ホームページ

{{Location map|Japan Osaka|lat_deg=34|lat_min=45|lat_sec=28.60|lon_deg=135|lon_min=38|lon_sec=46.87|label=太秦古墳群|position=top|width=220|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{Center|太秦古墳群の位置}}}} '''太秦古墳群'''（うずまさこふんぐん）は、[[大阪府]][[寝屋川市]]東部の丘陵上にある[[古墳群]]（初期[[群集墳]]）。現在では太秦高塚古墳（寝屋川市指定史跡）以外の墳丘は失われている。 == 概要== [[File:Uzumasa Kofun Group-18, funkyu.jpg|thumb|220px|right|18号墳 再現墳丘]] [[ファイル:太秦古墳群 出土品.JPG|thumb|220px|right|出土品
{{small|[[大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館]]企画展示時に撮影。}}]] 寝屋川市東部の太秦高塚町、国守町とその周辺の丘陵には、[[円墳]]と考えられる太秦高塚古墳の存在が以前から知られていた。それ以外にも各所から[[埴輪]]や[[須恵器]]が出土しており、付近には小金塚、向ヒ塚、廻し塚、モロ塚、上ノ山、墓ノ山、松谷山、段谷山など古墳の存在を窺わせる「塚」や「山」のつく小字名が知られ、かつては、かなりの広がりのある古墳群が存在したようである。1962年には太秦熱田神社の裏山から牡鹿の頭部と考えられる埴輪が出土したり、1965年には太秦高塚古墳の南約300メートルで木棺直葬と考えられる主体部が発見され、鉄鏃、直刀などが出土している。これ以外にも三環鈴、金環、銅鏃、子持勾玉2、勾玉3、紡錘車2などが付近にあった古墳の出土遺物として知られている。これらを総称して太秦古墳群と呼ばれている西田(1994)p85.。京都と大阪を結ぶ、[[第二京阪道路]]の建設に伴って2001年から2003年にかけて大阪府文化財センターが道路予定地内の事前調査を行い、古墳時代中期から後期にかけての[[墳丘]]を削平された小規模な古墳25基を発掘している。これらの古墳は尾根上で検出されたことから太秦古墳群の尾支群と呼ばれることになった。また同じく2001年には、寝屋川市により太秦高塚古墳が[[史跡]]整備のため発掘調査されており、その全容がほぼ明らかにされている。 ===尾支群の調査=== [[第二京阪道路]]建設による事前調査で明らかにされたもので、尾根上に古墳の周溝と考えられる遺構が多数確認された。調査前は、ほとんど削られており、主体部が明らかにされたのは1基（3号墳）のみで木棺直葬であった。古墳の規模は1辺または直径が4－14メートルの範囲のものであった。遺物は各古墳の周溝から須恵器や[[土師器]]が出土しているが埴輪が出土したのは2基（8・17号墳）のみで[[円筒埴輪]]、朝顔形埴輪が出土している。これらの遺物から尾支群は5世紀中葉から6世紀前葉に築造されたと考えられる市本(2005)p1.。 ===太秦高塚古墳=== [[File:Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|thumb|太秦高塚古墳]] 尾支群の北西800メートルに位置する古墳で、太秦古墳群では墳丘が完全に残る唯一の古墳である。2001年に史跡整備に伴う発掘調査が実施された。墳丘は北西部に[[造り出し]]を有する直径37メートルを測る円墳であり、2段に築かれ、[[葺石]]はなかった。墳丘のテラスの部分には円筒埴輪列、造り出し部でも円筒埴輪列が確認されており、加えて人物、家、盾、鶏、水鳥などの[[形象埴輪]]が出土した。墳頂部では埋葬主体が確認され、短甲、鏃、斧、馬具などの[[副葬品]]が出土している。埴輪などから5世紀後半の築造と考えられる。同じ太秦古墳でも、この高塚古墳と尾支群とでは墳丘規模、出土遺物などで大きな格差があり、当時の階層を反映していると考えられる。群集墳を構成する集団のリーダーの墓が太秦高塚古墳、集団の構成員の墓が尾支群のような群小古墳ではないかと考えられる。 現在、太秦高塚古墳は寝屋川市指定史跡に指定され、「太秦高塚古墳公園」内に整備保存されている。 File:Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|墳頂 File:Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun, tsukuridashi-2.jpg|造出 == 文化財 == === 寝屋川市指定文化財 === * 有形文化財 ** 太秦古墳群出土鹿形埴輪（考古資料） - 寝屋川市立埋蔵文化財資料館保管。2000年（平成12年）11月3日指定[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html 大阪府内指定文化財一覧表]（大阪府ホームページ、平成30年11月21日更新版）の寝屋川市ファイルより。。 * 史跡 ** 太秦高塚古墳 - 1997年（平成9年）11月3日指定。 ==脚注== {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} ==参考文献== *西太敏秀「茨田の古墳」『楽しい古墳めぐり』松籟社　84-86頁 *市本芳三「太秦古墳群」『シンポジウム　大阪の初期古墳を考える』1-3頁　大阪府立弥生文化博物館　2006年 *『太秦古墳群　発掘調査成果　大阪の初期群集墳を考える』　編集　財団法人大阪文化財センター　2006年 ==関連項目== *[[長原古墳群]] ==外部リンク== {{Commonscat|Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun|太秦高塚古墳}} *[https://www.city.neyagawa.osaka.jp/organization_list/kyoiku_shakaikyoiku/bunkasport/bunkazai/namesagasu/1378193397977.html 太秦古墳群]、[https://www.city.neyagawa.osaka.jp/Recommend/bunkazai/neyagawashishiteibunkazai/1382432802559.html 太秦高塚古墳] - Presently, all tumuli except the..."
revision.text:
{{Location map|Japan Osaka|lat_deg=34|lat_min=45|lat_sec=28.60|lon_deg=135|lon_min=38|lon_sec=46.87|label=Uzumasa Kofun Group|position=top|width=220|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{center|Location of Uzumasa Kofun Group}}}} '''Uzumasa Kofun Group''' (太秦古墳群, Uzumasa Kofun-gun) refers to a cluster of ancient burial mounds (kofun) located on the eastern hills of {{ill|Neyagawa, Osaka|ja|寝屋川市}}, [[Osaka Prefecture]]. Presently, all tumuli except the Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun (a Neyagawa City Designated Historic Site) have been lost. == Overview == [[File:Uzumasa Kofun Group-18, funkyu.jpg|thumb|220px|right|Restored Mound of Kofun No. 18]] [[File:太秦古墳群 出土品.JPG|thumb|220px|right|Artifacts from the site
{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at the {{ill|Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}}.}}]] The Uzumasa Kofun Group once extended across the hills around Uzumasa Takatsuka Town and Kokumori Town. Besides the known Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun, fragments of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} have been unearthed from various locations in the area. The presence of place names such as Koganezuka, Mukaihizuka, Mawashizuka, Morozuka, Kaminoyama, Hakunoyama, Matsutanidaniyama, and Dandaniyama suggests a broad distribution of burial mounds in the past. Notably, in 1962, a deer-shaped haniwa was excavated from behind the Uzumasa Atsuta Shrine. In 1965, 300 meters south of Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun, the remains of a burial believed to involve direct wooden coffin interment were discovered, yielding iron arrowheads and straight swords. Additional artifacts such as triple-ring bells, gold rings, bronze arrowheads, comma-shaped beads, and spindle whorls have also been found. Collectively, these findings are referred to as the Uzumasa Kofun GroupNishida (1994), p. 85.. From 2001 to 2003, the {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Cultural Properties Center|ja|大阪府文化財センター}} conducted investigations in preparation for constructing the {{ill|Second Keihan Highway|ja|第二京阪道路}}, unearthing remains of 25 small burial mounds, mostly flattened, dating from the mid-5th to early 6th centuries. These mounds, located along a ridge, are referred to as the Uzumasa Kofun Group's Ridge Cluster. Additionally, Neyagawa City carried out excavations in 2001 for the preservation of Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun, fully uncovering its structure. === Ridge Cluster Investigations === Investigations for the {{ill|Second Keihan Highway|ja|第二京阪道路}} revealed numerous remains of mound ditches along a ridge. Most mounds were already damaged, with burial structures identified in only one (Kofun No. 3), which contained a direct wooden coffin burial. These mounds ranged in size from 4 to 14 meters in diameter or length. Artifacts such as Sue ware and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} were recovered from the surrounding ditches, while haniwa were found at only two sites (Kofun Nos. 8 and 17), yielding cylindrical and lotus-shaped haniwa. Based on these finds, the Ridge Cluster is dated to the mid-5th to early 6th centuriesIchimoto (2005), p. 1.. === Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun === [[File:Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|thumb|Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun]] Located 800 meters northwest of the Ridge Cluster, Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun is the only mound within the group that remains intact. Excavations in 2001 revealed a two-tiered, 37-meter-diameter circular mound with a northwestern {{ill|Tsukuridashi|ja|造り出し}} projection. The mound lacked stone coverings but had cylindrical haniwa rows on the terrace and Tsukuridashi. Figurative haniwa, including depictions of humans, houses, shields, roosters, and waterfowl, were also found. Artifacts such as armor, arrowheads, axes, and horse fittings were unearthed from the burial chamber at the mound's summit. These findings suggest the mound was constructed in the late 5th century. The significant differences in mound size and artifacts between Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun and the Ridge Cluster imply social stratification, with Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun representing a leader’s burial and the Ridge Cluster representing subordinate graves. Today, Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun is a Neyagawa City Designated Historic Site and is preserved within the Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun Park. File:Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Summit of the mound File:Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun, tsukuridashi-2.jpg|Tsukuridashi projection == Cultural Properties == === Neyagawa City Designated Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Properties ** Deer-shaped Haniwa from Uzumasa Kofun Group (Archaeological Artifacts) - Stored at the Neyagawa City Buried Cultural Properties Museum. Designated on November 3, 2000[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html List of Designated Cultural Properties in Osaka Prefecture] (Osaka Prefecture website, updated November 21, 2018).. * Historic Sites ** Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun - Designated on November 3, 1997. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Nishida Toshihide, "Kofun of Makimuku," in *Enjoyable Kofun Tour*, Shōraisha, pp. 84-86. * Ichimoto Yoshizo, "Uzumasa Kofun Group," in *Symposium: Understanding Osaka's Early Kofun*, Osaka Prefectural Yayoi Culture Museum, pp. 1-3, 2006. * *Uzumasa Kofun Group Excavation Results: Considering Osaka's Early Kofun Groups*, ed. Osaka Cultural Properties Center, 2006. == Related Topics == * [[Nagahara Kofun Group]] == External Links == {{Commonscat|Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun|Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun}} * [https://www.city.neyagawa.osaka.jp/organization_list/kyoiku_shakaikyoiku/bunkasport/bunkazai/namesagasu/1378193397977.html Uzumasa Kofun Group], [https://www.city.neyagawa.osaka.jp/Recommend/bunkazai/neyagawashishiteibunkazai/1382432802559.html Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun] - Neyagawa City Official Website [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Neyagawa]] [[Category:Cluster burial mounds]] [[ja:太秦古墳群]] [[ja:太秦古墳群]]

Additional artifacts such as triple-ring bells, gold rings, bronze arrowheads, comma-shaped beads, and spindle whorls have also been found. Collectively, these findings are referred to as the Uzumasa Kofun GroupNishida (1994), p. 85.. From 2001 to 2003, the {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Cultural Properties Center|ja|大阪府文化財センター}} conducted investigations in preparation for constructing the {{ill|Second Keihan Highway|ja|第二京阪道路}}, unearthing remains of 25 small burial mounds, mostly flattened, dating from the mid-5th to early 6th centuries. These mounds, located along a ridge, are referred to as the Uzumasa Kofun Group's Ridge Cluster. Additionally, Neyagawa City carried out excavations in 2001 for the preservation of Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun, fully uncovering its structure. === Ridge Cluster Investigations === Investigations for the {{ill|Second Keihan Highway|ja|第二京阪道路}} revealed numerous remains of mound ditches along a ridge. Most mounds were already damaged, with burial structures identified in only one (Kofun No. 3), which contained a direct wooden coffin burial. These mounds ranged in size from 4 to 14 meters in diameter or length. Artifacts such as Sue ware and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} were recovered from the surrounding ditches, while haniwa were found at only two sites (Kofun Nos. 8 and 17), yielding cylindrical and lotus-shaped haniwa. Based on these finds, the Ridge Cluster is dated to the mid-5th to early 6th centuriesIchimoto (2005), p. 1.. === Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun === [[File:Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|thumb|Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun]] Located 800 meters northwest of the Ridge Cluster, Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun is the only mound within the group that remains intact. Excavations in 2001 revealed a two-tiered, 37-meter-diameter circular mound with a northwestern {{ill|Tsukuridashi|ja|造り出し}} projection. The mound lacked stone coverings but had cylindrical haniwa rows on the terrace and Tsukuridashi. Figurative haniwa, including depictions of humans, houses, shields, roosters, and waterfowl, were also found. Artifacts such as armor, arrowheads, axes, and horse fittings were unearthed from the burial chamber at the mound's summit. These findings suggest the mound was constructed in the late 5th century. The significant differences in mound size and artifacts between Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun and the Ridge Cluster imply social stratification, with Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun representing a leader’s burial and the Ridge Cluster representing subordinate graves. Today, Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun is a Neyagawa City Designated Historic Site and is preserved within the Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun Park. File:Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Summit of the mound File:Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun, tsukuridashi-2.jpg|Tsukuridashi projection == Cultural Properties == === Neyagawa City Designated Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Properties ** Deer-shaped Haniwa from Uzumasa Kofun Group (Archaeological Artifacts) - Stored at the Neyagawa City Buried Cultural Properties Museum. Designated on November 3, 2000[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html List of Designated Cultural Properties in Osaka Prefecture] (Osaka Prefecture website, updated November 21, 2018).. * Historic Sites ** Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun - Designated on November 3, 1997. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Nishida Toshihide, "Kofun of Makimuku," in *Enjoyable Kofun Tour*, Shōraisha, pp. 84-86. * Ichimoto Yoshizo, "Uzumasa Kofun Group," in *Symposium: Understanding Osaka's Early Kofun*, Osaka Prefectural Yayoi Culture Museum, pp. 1-3, 2006. * *Uzumasa Kofun Group Excavation Results: Considering Osaka's Early Kofun Groups*, ed. Osaka Cultural Properties Center, 2006. == Related Topics == * {{ill|Nagahara Kofun Group|ja|長原古墳群}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun|Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun}} * [https://www.city.neyagawa.osaka.jp/organization_list/kyoiku_shakaikyoiku/bunkasport/bunkazai/namesagasu/1378193397977.html Uzumasa Kofun Group], [https://www.city.neyagawa.osaka.jp/Recommend/bunkazai/neyagawashishiteibunkazai/1382432802559.html Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun] - Neyagawa City Official Website [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Neyagawa]] [[Category:Cluster burial mounds]] [[Category:pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:pages with redlinks to fill]] [[ja:太秦古墳群]] [[Category:pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:pages with redlinks to fill]] [[ja:太秦古墳群]]

{{Location map|Japan Osaka|lat_deg=34|lat_min=45|lat_sec=28.60|lon_deg=135|lon_min=38|lon_sec=46.87|label=Uzumasa Kofun Group|position=top|width=220|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{center|Location of Uzumasa Kofun Group}}}} '''Uzumasa Kofun Group''' (太秦古墳群, Uzumasa Kofun-gun) refers to a cluster of ancient burial mounds (kofun) located on the eastern hills of {{ill|Neyagawa, Osaka|ja|寝屋川市}}, [[Osaka Prefecture]]. Presently, all tumuli except the Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun (a Neyagawa City Designated Historic Site) have been lost. == Overview == [[File:Uzumasa Kofun Group-18, funkyu.jpg|thumb|220px|right|Restored Mound of Kofun No. 18]] [[File:太秦古墳群 出土品.JPG|thumb|220px|right|Artifacts from the site

Based on these finds, the Ridge Cluster is dated to the mid-5th to early 6th centuriesIchimoto (2005), p. 1.. === Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun === [[File:Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|thumb|Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun]] Located 800 meters northwest of the Ridge Cluster, Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun is the only mound within the group that remains intact. Excavations in 2001 revealed a two-tiered, 37-meter-diameter circular mound with a northwestern {{ill|Tsukuridashi|ja|造り出し}} projection. The mound lacked stone coverings but had cylindrical haniwa rows on the terrace and Tsukuridashi. Figurative haniwa, including depictions of humans, houses, shields, roosters, and waterfowl, were also found. Artifacts such as armor, arrowheads, axes, and horse fittings were unearthed from the burial chamber at the mound's summit. These findings suggest the mound was constructed in the late 5th century. The significant differences in mound size and artifacts between Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun and the Ridge Cluster imply social stratification, with Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun representing a leader’s burial and the Ridge Cluster representing subordinate graves. Today, Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun is a Neyagawa City Designated Historic Site and is preserved within the Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun Park. Designated on November 3, 2000[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html List of Designated Cultural Properties in Osaka Prefecture] (Osaka Prefecture website, updated November 21, 2018).. * Historic Sites ** Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun - Designated on November 3, 1997. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Nishida Toshihide, "Kofun of Makimuku," in *Enjoyable Kofun Tour*, Shōraisha, pp. 84-86. * Ichimoto Yoshizo, "Uzumasa Kofun Group," in *Symposium: Understanding Osaka's Early Kofun*, Osaka Prefectural Yayoi Culture Museum, pp. 1-3, 2006. * *Uzumasa Kofun Group Excavation Results: Considering Osaka's Early Kofun Groups*, ed. Additionally, Neyagawa City carried out excavations in 2001 for the preservation of Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun, fully uncovering its structure. === Ridge Cluster Investigations === Investigations for the {{ill|Second Keihan Highway|ja|第二京阪道路}} revealed numerous remains of mound ditches along a ridge. Most mounds were already damaged, with burial structures identified in only one (Kofun No. 3), which contained a direct wooden coffin burial. These mounds ranged in size from 4 to 14 meters in diameter or length. Artifacts such as Sue ware and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} were recovered from the surrounding ditches, while haniwa were found at only two sites (Kofun Nos. 8 and 17), yielding cylindrical and lotus-shaped haniwa. Based on these finds, the Ridge Cluster is dated to the mid-5th to early 6th centuriesIchimoto (2005), p. 1.. === Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun === [[File:Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|thumb|Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun]] Located 800 meters northwest of the Ridge Cluster, Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun is the only mound within the group that remains intact. Excavations in 2001 revealed a two-tiered, 37-meter-diameter circular mound with a northwestern {{ill|Tsukuridashi|ja|造り出し}} projection. The mound lacked stone coverings but had cylindrical haniwa rows on the terrace and Tsukuridashi. Figurative haniwa, including depictions of humans, houses, shields, roosters, and waterfowl, were also found. Artifacts such as armor, arrowheads, axes, and horse fittings were unearthed from the burial chamber at the mound's summit. These findings suggest the mound was constructed in the late 5th century. The significant differences in mound size and artifacts between Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun and the Ridge Cluster imply social stratification, with Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun representing a leader’s burial and the Ridge Cluster representing subordinate graves. Today, Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun is a Neyagawa City Designated Historic Site and is preserved within the Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun Park. File:Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Summit of the mound File:Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun, tsukuridashi-2.jpg|Tsukuridashi projection == Cultural Properties == === Neyagawa City Designated Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Properties ''' Deer-shaped Haniwa from Uzumasa Kofun Group (Archaeological Artifacts) - Stored at the Neyagawa City Buried Cultural Properties Museum. Designated on November 3, 2000[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html List of Designated Cultural Properties in Osaka Prefecture] (Osaka Prefecture website, updated November 21, 2018).. * Historic Sites ''' Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun - Designated on November 3, 1997. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Nishida Toshihide, "Kofun of Makimuku," in *Enjoyable Kofun Tour*, Shōraisha, pp. 84-86. * Ichimoto Yoshizo, "Uzumasa Kofun Group," in *Symposium: Understanding Osaka's Early Kofun*, Osaka Prefectural Yayoi Culture Museum, pp. 1-3, 2006. * *Uzumasa Kofun Group Excavation Results: Considering Osaka's Early Kofun Groups*, ed. Osaka Cultural Properties Center, 2006. == Related Topics == * {{ill|Nagahara Kofun Group|ja|長原古墳群}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun|Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun}} * [https://www.city.neyagawa.osaka.jp/organization_list/kyoiku_shakaikyoiku/bunkasport/bunkazai/namesagasu/1378193397977.html Uzumasa Kofun Group], [https://www.city.neyagawa.osaka.jp/Recommend/bunkazai/neyagawashishiteibunkazai/1382432802559.html Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun] - Neyagawa City Official Website [[Category:new pages]][[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:new pages]][[Category:History of Neyagawa]] [[Category:new pages]][[Category:Cluster burial mounds]] [[Category:new pages]][[Category:pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:new pages]][[Category:pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:new pages]][[Category:new pages]]太秦古墳群]] [[Category:new pages]][[Category:pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:new pages]][[Category:pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:new pages]][[Category:pages to try to fix ills]] [[Category:new pages]][[Category:new pages]]太秦古墳群]]

{{Location map|Japan Osaka|lat_deg=34|lat_min=45|lat_sec=28.60|lon_deg=135|lon_min=38|lon_sec=46.87|label=Uzumasa Kofun Group|position=top|width=220|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{center|Location of Uzumasa Kofun Group}}}} '''Uzumasa Kofun Group''' (太秦古墳群, Uzumasa Kofun-gun) refers to a cluster of ancient burial mounds (kofun) located on the eastern hills of {{ill|Neyagawa, Osaka|ja|寝屋川市}}, [[Osaka Prefecture]]. Presently, all tumuli except the Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun (a Neyagawa City Designated Historic Site) have been lost. == Overview == [[File:Uzumasa Kofun Group-18, funkyu.jpg|thumb|220px|right|Restored Mound of Kofun No. 18]] [[File:太秦古墳群 出土品.JPG|thumb|220px|right|Artifacts from the site
{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at the {{ill|Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}}.}}]] The Uzumasa Kofun Group once extended across the hills around Uzumasa Takatsuka Town and Kokumori Town. Besides the known Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun, fragments of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} have been unearthed from various locations in the area. The presence of place names such as Koganezuka, Mukaihizuka, Mawashizuka, Morozuka, Kaminoyama, Hakunoyama, Matsutanidaniyama, and Dandaniyama suggests a broad distribution of burial mounds in the past. Notably, in 1962, a deer-shaped haniwa was excavated from behind the Uzumasa Atsuta Shrine. In 1965, 300 meters south of Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun, the remains of a burial believed to involve direct wooden coffin interment were discovered, yielding iron arrowheads and straight swords. Additional artifacts such as triple-ring bells, gold rings, bronze arrowheads, comma-shaped beads, and spindle whorls have also been found. Collectively, these findings are referred to as the Uzumasa Kofun GroupNishida (1994), p. 85.. From 2001 to 2003, the {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Cultural Properties Center|ja|大阪府文化財センター}} conducted investigations in preparation for constructing the {{ill|Second Keihan Highway|ja|第二京阪道路}}, unearthing remains of 25 small burial mounds, mostly flattened, dating from the mid-5th to early 6th centuries. These mounds, located along a ridge, are referred to as the Uzumasa Kofun Group's Ridge Cluster. Additionally, Neyagawa City carried out excavations in 2001 for the preservation of Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun, fully uncovering its structure. === Ridge Cluster Investigations === Investigations for the {{ill|Second Keihan Highway|ja|第二京阪道路}} revealed numerous remains of mound ditches along a ridge. Most mounds were already damaged, with burial structures identified in only one (Kofun No. 3), which contained a direct wooden coffin burial. These mounds ranged in size from 4 to 14 meters in diameter or length. Artifacts such as Sue ware and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} were recovered from the surrounding ditches, while haniwa were found at only two sites (Kofun Nos. 8 and 17), yielding cylindrical and lotus-shaped haniwa. Based on these finds, the Ridge Cluster is dated to the mid-5th to early 6th centuriesIchimoto (2005), p. 1.. === Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun === [[File:Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|thumb|Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun]] Located 800 meters northwest of the Ridge Cluster, Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun is the only mound within the group that remains intact. Excavations in 2001 revealed a two-tiered, 37-meter-diameter circular mound with a northwestern {{ill|Tsukuridashi|ja|造り出し}} projection. The mound lacked stone coverings but had cylindrical haniwa rows on the terrace and Tsukuridashi. Figurative haniwa, including depictions of humans, houses, shields, roosters, and waterfowl, were also found. Artifacts such as armor, arrowheads, axes, and horse fittings were unearthed from the burial chamber at the mound's summit. These findings suggest the mound was constructed in the late 5th century. The significant differences in mound size and artifacts between Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun and the Ridge Cluster imply social stratification, with Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun representing a leader’s burial and the Ridge Cluster representing subordinate graves. Today, Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun is a Neyagawa City Designated Historic Site and is preserved within the Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun Park. File:Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Summit of the mound File:Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun, tsukuridashi-2.jpg|Tsukuridashi projection == Cultural Properties == === Neyagawa City Designated Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Properties ''' Deer-shaped Haniwa from Uzumasa Kofun Group (Archaeological Artifacts) - Stored at the Neyagawa City Buried Cultural Properties Museum. Designated on November 3, 2000[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html List of Designated Cultural Properties in Osaka Prefecture] (Osaka Prefecture website, updated November 21, 2018).. * Historic Sites ''' Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun - Designated on November 3, 1997. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Nishida Toshihide, "Kofun of Makimuku," in *Enjoyable Kofun Tour*, Shōraisha, pp. 84-86. * Ichimoto Yoshizo, "Uzumasa Kofun Group," in *Symposium: Understanding Osaka's Early Kofun*, Osaka Prefectural Yayoi Culture Museum, pp. 1-3, 2006. * *Uzumasa Kofun Group Excavation Results: Considering Osaka's Early Kofun Groups*, ed. Osaka Cultural Properties Center, 2006. == Related Topics == * {{ill|Nagahara Kofun Group|ja|長原古墳群}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun|Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun}} * [https://www.city.neyagawa.osaka.jp/organization_list/kyoiku_shakaikyoiku/bunkasport/bunkazai/namesagasu/1378193397977.html Uzumasa Kofun Group], [https://www.city.neyagawa.osaka.jp/Recommend/bunkazai/neyagawashishiteibunkazai/1382432802559.html Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun] - Neyagawa City Official Website [[Category:new pages]][[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:new pages]][[Category:History of Neyagawa]] [[Category:new pages]][[Category:Cluster burial mounds]] [[Category:new pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Uzumasa Kofun Cluster}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:new pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Uzumasa Kofun Cluster}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:new pages]][[Category:new pages]]太秦古墳群]] [[Category:new pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Uzumasa Kofun Cluster}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:new pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Uzumasa Kofun Cluster}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:new pages]][[Category:pages to try to fix ills]] [[Category:new pages]][[Category:new pages]]太秦古墳群]]

{{Location map|Japan Osaka|lat_deg=34|lat_min=45|lat_sec=28.60|lon_deg=135|lon_min=38|lon_sec=46.87|label=Uzumasa Kofun Group|position=top|width=220|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{center|Location of Uzumasa Kofun Group}}}} '''Uzumasa Kofun Group''' (太秦古墳群, Uzumasa Kofun-gun) refers to a cluster of ancient burial mounds (kofun) located on the eastern hills of {{ill|Neyagawa, Osaka|ja|寝屋川市}}, [[Osaka Prefecture]]. Presently, all tumuli except the Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun (a Neyagawa City Designated Historic Site) have been lost. == Overview == [[File:Uzumasa Kofun Group-18, funkyu.jpg|thumb|220px|right|Restored Mound of Kofun No. 18]] [[File:太秦古墳群 出土品.JPG|thumb|220px|right|Artifacts from the site
{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at the {{ill|Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}}.}}]] The Uzumasa Kofun Group once extended across the hills around Uzumasa Takatsuka Town and Kokumori Town. Besides the known Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun, fragments of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} have been unearthed from various locations in the area. The presence of place names such as Koganezuka, Mukaihizuka, Mawashizuka, Morozuka, Kaminoyama, Hakunoyama, Matsutanidaniyama, and Dandaniyama suggests a broad distribution of burial mounds in the past. Notably, in 1962, a deer-shaped haniwa was excavated from behind the Uzumasa Atsuta Shrine. In 1965, 300 meters south of Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun, the remains of a burial believed to involve direct wooden coffin interment were discovered, yielding iron arrowheads and straight swords. Additional artifacts such as triple-ring bells, gold rings, bronze arrowheads, comma-shaped beads, and spindle whorls have also been found. Collectively, these findings are referred to as the Uzumasa Kofun GroupNishida (1994), p. 85.. From 2001 to 2003, the {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Cultural Properties Center|ja|大阪府文化財センター}} conducted investigations in preparation for constructing the {{ill|Second Keihan Highway|ja|第二京阪道路}}, unearthing remains of 25 small burial mounds, mostly flattened, dating from the mid-5th to early 6th centuries. These mounds, located along a ridge, are referred to as the Uzumasa Kofun Group's Ridge Cluster. Additionally, Neyagawa City carried out excavations in 2001 for the preservation of Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun, fully uncovering its structure. === Ridge Cluster Investigations === Investigations for the {{ill|Second Keihan Highway|ja|第二京阪道路}} revealed numerous remains of mound ditches along a ridge. Most mounds were already damaged, with burial structures identified in only one (Kofun No. 3), which contained a direct wooden coffin burial. These mounds ranged in size from 4 to 14 meters in diameter or length. Artifacts such as Sue ware and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} were recovered from the surrounding ditches, while haniwa were found at only two sites (Kofun Nos. 8 and 17), yielding cylindrical and lotus-shaped haniwa. Based on these finds, the Ridge Cluster is dated to the mid-5th to early 6th centuriesIchimoto (2005), p. 1.. === Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun === [[File:Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|thumb|Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun]] Located 800 meters northwest of the Ridge Cluster, Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun is the only mound within the group that remains intact. Excavations in 2001 revealed a two-tiered, 37-meter-diameter circular mound with a northwestern {{ill|Tsukuridashi|ja|造り出し}} projection. The mound lacked stone coverings but had cylindrical haniwa rows on the terrace and Tsukuridashi. Figurative haniwa, including depictions of humans, houses, shields, roosters, and waterfowl, were also found. Artifacts such as armor, arrowheads, axes, and horse fittings were unearthed from the burial chamber at the mound's summit. These findings suggest the mound was constructed in the late 5th century. The significant differences in mound size and artifacts between Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun and the Ridge Cluster imply social stratification, with Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun representing a leader’s burial and the Ridge Cluster representing subordinate graves. Today, Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun is a Neyagawa City Designated Historic Site and is preserved within the Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun Park. File:Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Summit of the mound File:Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun, tsukuridashi-2.jpg|Tsukuridashi projection == Cultural Properties == === Neyagawa City Designated Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Properties ''' Deer-shaped Haniwa from Uzumasa Kofun Group (Archaeological Artifacts) - Stored at the Neyagawa City Buried Cultural Properties Museum. Designated on November 3, 2000[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html List of Designated Cultural Properties in Osaka Prefecture] (Osaka Prefecture website, updated November 21, 2018).. * Historic Sites ''' Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun - Designated on November 3, 1997. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Nishida Toshihide, "Kofun of Makimuku," in *Enjoyable Kofun Tour*, Shōraisha, pp. 84-86. * Ichimoto Yoshizo, "Uzumasa Kofun Group," in *Symposium: Understanding Osaka's Early Kofun*, Osaka Prefectural Yayoi Culture Museum, pp. 1-3, 2006. * *Uzumasa Kofun Group Excavation Results: Considering Osaka's Early Kofun Groups*, ed. Osaka Cultural Properties Center, 2006. == Related Topics == * {{ill|Nagahara Kofun Group|ja|長原古墳群}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun|Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun}} * [https://www.city.neyagawa.osaka.jp/organization_list/kyoiku_shakaikyoiku/bunkasport/bunkazai/namesagasu/1378193397977.html Uzumasa Kofun Group], [https://www.city.neyagawa.osaka.jp/Recommend/bunkazai/neyagawashishiteibunkazai/1382432802559.html Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun] - Neyagawa City Official Website [[Category:new pages]][[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:new pages]][[Category:History of Neyagawa]] [[Category:new pages]][[Category:Cluster burial mounds]] [[Category:new pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Uzumasa Kofun Cluster}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:new pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Uzumasa Kofun Cluster}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:new pages]][[Category:new pages]]太秦古墳群]] [[Category:new pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Uzumasa Kofun Cluster}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:new pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Uzumasa Kofun Cluster}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:new pages]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:kk]][[category:kk]] 太秦古墳群]]

The presence of place names such as Koganezuka, Mukaihizuka, Mawashizuka, Morozuka, Kaminoyama, Hakunoyama, Matsutanidaniyama, and Dandaniyama suggests a broad distribution of burial mounds in the past. Notably, in 1962, a deer-shaped haniwa was excavated from behind the Uzumasa Atsuta Shrine. In 1965, 300 meters south of Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun, the remains of a burial believed to involve direct wooden coffin interment were discovered, yielding iron arrowheads and straight swords. Additional artifacts such as triple-ring bells, gold rings, bronze arrowheads, comma-shaped beads, and spindle whorls have also been found. Collectively, these findings are referred to as the Uzumasa Kofun GroupNishida (1994), p. 85.. From 2001 to 2003, the {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Cultural Properties Center|ja|大阪府文化財センター}} conducted investigations in preparation for constructing the {{ill|Second Keihan Highway|ja|第二京阪道路}}, unearthing remains of 25 small burial mounds, mostly flattened, dating from the mid-5th to early 6th centuries. These mounds, located along a ridge, are referred to as the Uzumasa Kofun Group's Ridge Cluster. Additionally, Neyagawa City carried out excavations in 2001 for the preservation of Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun, fully uncovering its structure. === Ridge Cluster Investigations === Investigations for the {{ill|Second Keihan Highway|ja|第二京阪道路}} revealed numerous remains of mound ditches along a ridge. Most mounds were already damaged, with burial structures identified in only one (Kofun No. 3), which contained a direct wooden coffin burial. These mounds ranged in size from 4 to 14 meters in diameter or length. Artifacts such as Sue ware and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} were recovered from the surrounding ditches, while haniwa were found at only two sites (Kofun Nos. 8 and 17), yielding cylindrical and lotus-shaped haniwa. Based on these finds, the Ridge Cluster is dated to the mid-5th to early 6th centuriesIchimoto (2005), p. 1.. === Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun === [[File:Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|thumb|Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun]] Located 800 meters northwest of the Ridge Cluster, Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun is the only mound within the group that remains intact. Excavations in 2001 revealed a two-tiered, 37-meter-diameter circular mound with a northwestern {{ill|Tsukuridashi|ja|造り出し}} projection. The mound lacked stone coverings but had cylindrical haniwa rows on the terrace and Tsukuridashi. Figurative haniwa, including depictions of humans, houses, shields, roosters, and waterfowl, were also found. Artifacts such as armor, arrowheads, axes, and horse fittings were unearthed from the burial chamber at the mound's summit. These findings suggest the mound was constructed in the late 5th century. The significant differences in mound size and artifacts between Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun and the Ridge Cluster imply social stratification, with Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun representing a leader’s burial and the Ridge Cluster representing subordinate graves. Today, Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun is a Neyagawa City Designated Historic Site and is preserved within the Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun Park. File:Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Summit of the mound File:Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun, tsukuridashi-2.jpg|Tsukuridashi projection == Cultural Properties == === Neyagawa City Designated Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Properties ''' Deer-shaped Haniwa from Uzumasa Kofun Group (Archaeological Artifacts) - Stored at the Neyagawa City Buried Cultural Properties Museum. Designated on November 3, 2000[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html List of Designated Cultural Properties in Osaka Prefecture] (Osaka Prefecture website, updated November 21, 2018).. * Historic Sites ''' Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun - Designated on November 3, 1997. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Nishida Toshihide, "Kofun of Makimuku," in *Enjoyable Kofun Tour*, Shōraisha, pp. 84-86. * Ichimoto Yoshizo, "Uzumasa Kofun Group," in *Symposium: Understanding Osaka's Early Kofun*, Osaka Prefectural Yayoi Culture Museum, pp. 1-3, 2006. * *Uzumasa Kofun Group Excavation Results: Considering Osaka's Early Kofun Groups*, ed. Osaka Cultural Properties Center, 2006. == Related Topics == * {{ill|Nagahara Kofun Group|ja|長原古墳群}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun|Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun}} * {{Location map|Japan Osaka|lat_deg=34|lat_min=45|lat_sec=28.60|lon_deg=135|lon_min=38|lon_sec=46.87|label=Uzumasa Kofun Group|position=top|width=220|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{center|Location of Uzumasa Kofun Group}}}} '''Uzumasa Kofun Group''' (太秦古墳群, Uzumasa Kofun-gun) refers to a cluster of ancient burial mounds (kofun) located on the eastern hills of {{ill|Neyagawa, Osaka|ja|寝屋川市}}, [[Osaka Prefecture]]. Presently, all tumuli except the Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun (a Neyagawa City Designated Historic Site) have been lost. == Overview == [[File:Uzumasa Kofun Group-18, funkyu.jpg|thumb|220px|right|Restored Mound of Kofun No. 18]] [[File:太秦古墳群 出土品.JPG|thumb|220px|right|Artifacts from the site
{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at the {{ill|Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}}.}}]] The Uzumasa Kofun Group once extended across the hills around Uzumasa Takatsuka Town and Kokumori Town. Besides the known Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun, fragments of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} have been unearthed from various locations in the area. The presence of place names such as Koganezuka, Mukaihizuka, Mawashizuka, Morozuka, Kaminoyama, Hakunoyama, Matsutanidaniyama, and Dandaniyama suggests a broad distribution of burial mounds in the past. From 2001 to 2003, the {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Cultural Properties Center|ja|大阪府文化財センター}} conducted investigations in preparation for constructing the {{ill|Second Keihan Highway|ja|第二京阪道路}}, unearthing remains of 25 small burial mounds, mostly flattened, dating from the mid-5th to early 6th centuries. These mounds, located along a ridge, are referred to as the Uzumasa Kofun Group's Ridge Cluster. Most mounds were already damaged, with burial structures identified in only one (Kofun No. 3), which contained a direct wooden coffin burial. These mounds ranged in size from 4 to 14 meters in diameter or length. Artifacts such as Sue ware and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} were recovered from the surrounding ditches, while haniwa were found at only two sites (Kofun Nos. 8 and 17), yielding cylindrical and lotus-shaped haniwa. Based on these finds, the Ridge Cluster is dated to the mid-5th to early 6th centuriesIchimoto (2005), p. 1.. === Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun === [[File:Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|thumb|Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun]] Located 800 meters northwest of the Ridge Cluster, Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun is the only mound within the group that remains intact. Excavations in 2001 revealed a two-tiered, 37-meter-diameter circular mound with a northwestern {{ill|Tsukuridashi|ja|造り出し}} projection. The mound lacked stone coverings but had cylindrical haniwa rows on the terrace and Tsukuridashi. Figurative haniwa, including depictions of humans, houses, shields, roosters, and waterfowl, were also found. Artifacts such as armor, arrowheads, axes, and horse fittings were unearthed from the burial chamber at the mound's summit. These findings suggest the mound was constructed in the late 5th century. The significant differences in mound size and artifacts between Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun and the Ridge Cluster imply social stratification, with Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun representing a leader’s burial and the Ridge Cluster representing subordinate graves. Today, Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun is a Neyagawa City Designated Historic Site and is preserved within the Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun Park. File:Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Summit of the mound File:Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun, tsukuridashi-2.jpg|Tsukuridashi projection == Cultural Properties == === Neyagawa City Designated Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Properties ''' Deer-shaped Haniwa from Uzumasa Kofun Group (Archaeological Artifacts) - Stored at the Neyagawa City Buried Cultural Properties Museum. Designated on November 3, 2000[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html List of Designated Cultural Properties in Osaka Prefecture] (Osaka Prefecture website, updated November 21, 2018).. * Historic Sites ''' Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun - Designated on November 3, 1997. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Nishida Toshihide, "Kofun of Makimuku," in *Enjoyable Kofun Tour*, Shōraisha, pp. 84-86. * Ichimoto Yoshizo, "Uzumasa Kofun Group," in *Symposium: Understanding Osaka's Early Kofun*, Osaka Prefectural Yayoi Culture Museum, pp. 1-3, 2006. * *Uzumasa Kofun Group Excavation Results: Considering Osaka's Early Kofun Groups*, ed. Osaka Cultural Properties Center, 2006. == Related Topics == * {{ill|Nagahara Kofun Group|ja|長原古墳群}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun|Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun}} * [https://www.city.neyagawa.osaka.jp/organization_list/kyoiku_shakaikyoiku/bunkasport/bunkazai/namesagasu/1378193397977.html Uzumasa Kofun Group], [https://www.city.neyagawa.osaka.jp/Recommend/bunkazai/neyagawashishiteibunkazai/1382432802559.html Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun] - Neyagawa City Official Website [[Category:new pages]][[ja:[[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:new pages]][[ja:[[Category:History of Neyagawa]] [[Category:new pages]][[ja:[[Category:Cluster burial mounds]] [[Category:new pages]][[ja:[[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:kkk]][[ja:{{DEFAULTSORT:Uzumasa Kofun Cluster}}[[Category:kk]][[ja:[[Category:new pages]][[ja:[[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:kkk]][[ja:{{DEFAULTSORT:Uzumasa Kofun Cluster}}[[Category:kk]][[ja:[[Category:new pages]][[ja:[[Category:new pages]][[ja:太秦古墳群]] [[Category:new pages]][[ja:[[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:kkk]][[ja:{{DEFAULTSORT:Uzumasa Kofun Cluster}}[[Category:kk]][[ja:[[Category:new pages]][[ja:[[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:kkk]][[ja:{{DEFAULTSORT:Uzumasa Kofun Cluster}}[[Category:kk]][[ja:[[Category:new pages]][[ja:[[Category:new pages]][[ja:[[Category:new pages]][[ja:[[Category:kk]][[ja:[[Category:kk]] [[Category:test if the jawiki is actually present]] [[ja:太秦古墳群]]

Designated on November 3, 2000[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html List of Designated Cultural Properties in Osaka Prefecture] (Osaka Prefecture website, updated November 21, 2018).. * Historic Sites ''' Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun - Designated on November 3, 1997. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Nishida Toshihide, "Kofun of Makimuku," in *Enjoyable Kofun Tour*, Shōraisha, pp. 84-86. * Ichimoto Yoshizo, "Uzumasa Kofun Group," in *Symposium: Understanding Osaka's Early Kofun*, Osaka Prefectural Yayoi Culture Museum, pp. 1-3, 2006. * *Uzumasa Kofun Group Excavation Results: Considering Osaka's Early Kofun Groups*, ed. revision.text:
{{Location map|Japan Osaka|lat_deg=34|lat_min=45|lat_sec=28.60|lon_deg=135|lon_min=38|lon_sec=46.87|label=Uzumasa Kofun Group|position=top|width=220|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{center|Location of Uzumasa Kofun Group}}}} '''Uzumasa Kofun Group''' (太秦古墳群, Uzumasa Kofun-gun) refers to a cluster of ancient burial mounds (kofun) located on the eastern hills of {{ill|Neyagawa, Osaka|ja|寝屋川市}}, [[Osaka Prefecture]]. Presently, all tumuli except the Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun (a Neyagawa City Designated Historic Site) have been lost. == Overview == [[File:Uzumasa Kofun Group-18, funkyu.jpg|thumb|220px|right|Restored Mound of Kofun No. 18]] [[File:太秦古墳群 出土品.JPG|thumb|220px|right|Artifacts from the site
{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at the {{ill|Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}}.}}]] The Uzumasa Kofun Group once extended across the hills around Uzumasa Takatsuka Town and Kokumori Town. Besides the known Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun, fragments of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} have been unearthed from various locations in the area. The presence of place names such as Koganezuka, Mukaihizuka, Mawashizuka, Morozuka, Kaminoyama, Hakunoyama, Matsutanidaniyama, and Dandaniyama suggests a broad distribution of burial mounds in the past. Notably, in 1962, a deer-shaped haniwa was excavated from behind the Uzumasa Atsuta Shrine. In 1965, 300 meters south of Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun, the remains of a burial believed to involve direct wooden coffin interment were discovered, yielding iron arrowheads and straight swords. Additional artifacts such as triple-ring bells, gold rings, bronze arrowheads, comma-shaped beads, and spindle whorls have also been found. Collectively, these findings are referred to as the Uzumasa Kofun GroupNishida (1994), p. 85.. From 2001 to 2003, the {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Cultural Properties Center|ja|大阪府文化財センター}} conducted investigations in preparation for constructing the {{ill|Second Keihan Highway|ja|第二京阪道路}}, unearthing remains of 25 small burial mounds, mostly flattened, dating from the mid-5th to early 6th centuries. These mounds, located along a ridge, are referred to as the Uzumasa Kofun Group's Ridge Cluster. Additionally, Neyagawa City carried out excavations in 2001 for the preservation of Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun, fully uncovering its structure. === Ridge Cluster Investigations === Investigations for the {{ill|Second Keihan Highway|ja|第二京阪道路}} revealed numerous remains of mound ditches along a ridge. Most mounds were already damaged, with burial structures identified in only one (Kofun No. 3), which contained a direct wooden coffin burial. These mounds ranged in size from 4 to 14 meters in diameter or length. Artifacts such as Sue ware and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} were recovered from the surrounding ditches, while haniwa were found at only two sites (Kofun Nos. 8 and 17), yielding cylindrical and lotus-shaped haniwa. Based on these finds, the Ridge Cluster is dated to the mid-5th to early 6th centuriesIchimoto (2005), p. 1.. === Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun === [[File:Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|thumb|Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun]] Located 800 meters northwest of the Ridge Cluster, Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun is the only mound within the group that remains intact. Excavations in 2001 revealed a two-tiered, 37-meter-diameter circular mound with a northwestern {{ill|Tsukuridashi|ja|造り出し}} projection. The mound lacked stone coverings but had cylindrical haniwa rows on the terrace and Tsukuridashi. Figurative haniwa, including depictions of humans, houses, shields, roosters, and waterfowl, were also found. Artifacts such as armor, arrowheads, axes, and horse fittings were unearthed from the burial chamber at the mound's summit. These findings suggest the mound was constructed in the late 5th century. The significant differences in mound size and artifacts between Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun and the Ridge Cluster imply social stratification, with Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun representing a leader’s burial and the Ridge Cluster representing subordinate graves. Today, Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun is a Neyagawa City Designated Historic Site and is preserved within the Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun Park. File:Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Summit of the mound File:Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun, tsukuridashi-2.jpg|Tsukuridashi projection == Cultural Properties == === Neyagawa City Designated Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Properties ''' Deer-shaped Haniwa from Uzumasa Kofun Group (Archaeological Artifacts) - Stored at the Neyagawa City Buried Cultural Properties Museum. Designated on November 3, 2000[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html List of Designated Cultural Properties in Osaka Prefecture] (Osaka Prefecture website, updated November 21, 2018).. * Historic Sites ''' Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun - Designated on November 3, 1997. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Nishida Toshihide, "Kofun of Makimuku," in *Enjoyable Kofun Tour*, Shōraisha, pp. 84-86. * Ichimoto Yoshizo, "Uzumasa Kofun Group," in *Symposium: Understanding Osaka's Early Kofun*, Osaka Prefectural Yayoi Culture Museum, pp. 1-3, 2006. * *Uzumasa Kofun Group Excavation Results: Considering Osaka's Early Kofun Groups*, ed. Osaka Cultural Properties Center, 2006. == Related Topics == * {{ill|Nagahara Kofun Group|ja|長原古墳群}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun|Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun}} * [https://www.city.neyagawa.osaka.jp/organization_list/kyoiku_shakaikyoiku/bunkasport/bunkazai/namesagasu/1378193397977.html Uzumasa Kofun Group], [https://www.city.neyagawa.osaka.jp/Recommend/bunkazai/neyagawashishiteibunkazai/1382432802559.html Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun] - Neyagawa City Official Website [[Category:new pages]] [[Category:new pages]] [[Category:new pages]] [[Category:new pages]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:History of Neyagawa]] [[Category:kkk]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:kkk]] [[Category:Cluster burial mounds]] [[Category:new pages]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:kkk]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:kkk]] [[ja:太秦古墳群]]

Osaka Cultural Properties Center, 2006. == Related Topics == * {{ill|Nagahara Kofun Group|ja|長原古墳群}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun|Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun}} * [https://www.city.neyagawa.osaka.jp/organization_list/kyoiku_shakaikyoiku/bunkasport/bunkazai/namesagasu/1378193397977.html Uzumasa Kofun Group], [https://www.city.neyagawa.osaka.jp/Recommend/bunkazai/neyagawashishiteibunkazai/1382432802559.html Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun] - Neyagawa City Official Website [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:History of Neyagawa]] [[Category:kkk]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:kkk]] [[Category:Cluster burial mounds]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:kkk]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:kkk]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Uzumasa Kofun Cluster]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:original pages]][[category:Uzumasa Kofun Cluster]][[category:original pages]] [[ja:太秦古墳群]]

{{Location map|Japan Osaka|lat_deg=34|lat_min=45|lat_sec=28.60|lon_deg=135|lon_min=38|lon_sec=46.87|label=Uzumasa Kofun Group|position=top|width=220|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{center|Location of Uzumasa Kofun Group}}}} '''Uzumasa Kofun Group''' (太秦古墳群, Uzumasa Kofun-gun) refers to a cluster of ancient burial mounds (kofun) located on the eastern hills of {{ill|Neyagawa, Osaka|ja|寝屋川市}}, [[Osaka Prefecture]]. Presently, all tumuli except the Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun (a Neyagawa City Designated Historic Site) have been lost. == Overview == [[File:Uzumasa Kofun Group-18, funkyu.jpg|thumb|220px|right|Restored Mound of Kofun No. 18]] [[File:太秦古墳群 出土品.JPG|thumb|220px|right|Artifacts from the site
{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at the {{ill|Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}}.}}]] The Uzumasa Kofun Group once extended across the hills around Uzumasa Takatsuka Town and Kokumori Town. Besides the known Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun, fragments of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} have been unearthed from various locations in the area. The presence of place names such as Koganezuka, Mukaihizuka, Mawashizuka, Morozuka, Kaminoyama, Hakunoyama, Matsutanidaniyama, and Dandaniyama suggests a broad distribution of burial mounds in the past. Notably, in 1962, a deer-shaped haniwa was excavated from behind the Uzumasa Atsuta Shrine. In 1965, 300 meters south of Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun, the remains of a burial believed to involve direct wooden coffin interment were discovered, yielding iron arrowheads and straight swords. Additional artifacts such as triple-ring bells, gold rings, bronze arrowheads, comma-shaped beads, and spindle whorls have also been found. Collectively, these findings are referred to as the Uzumasa Kofun GroupNishida (1994), p. 85.. From 2001 to 2003, the {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Cultural Properties Center|ja|大阪府文化財センター}} conducted investigations in preparation for constructing the {{ill|Second Keihan Highway|ja|第二京阪道路}}, unearthing remains of 25 small burial mounds, mostly flattened, dating from the mid-5th to early 6th centuries. These mounds, located along a ridge, are referred to as the Uzumasa Kofun Group's Ridge Cluster. Additionally, Neyagawa City carried out excavations in 2001 for the preservation of Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun, fully uncovering its structure. === Ridge Cluster Investigations === Investigations for the {{ill|Second Keihan Highway|ja|第二京阪道路}} revealed numerous remains of mound ditches along a ridge. Most mounds were already damaged, with burial structures identified in only one (Kofun No. 3), which contained a direct wooden coffin burial. These mounds ranged in size from 4 to 14 meters in diameter or length. Artifacts such as Sue ware and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} were recovered from the surrounding ditches, while haniwa were found at only two sites (Kofun Nos. 8 and 17), yielding cylindrical and lotus-shaped haniwa. Based on these finds, the Ridge Cluster is dated to the mid-5th to early 6th centuriesIchimoto (2005), p. 1.. === Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun === [[File:Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|thumb|Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun]] Located 800 meters northwest of the Ridge Cluster, Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun is the only mound within the group that remains intact. Excavations in 2001 revealed a two-tiered, 37-meter-diameter circular mound with a northwestern {{ill|Tsukuridashi|ja|造り出し}} projection. The mound lacked stone coverings but had cylindrical haniwa rows on the terrace and Tsukuridashi. Figurative haniwa, including depictions of humans, houses, shields, roosters, and waterfowl, were also found. Artifacts such as armor, arrowheads, axes, and horse fittings were unearthed from the burial chamber at the mound's summit. These findings suggest the mound was constructed in the late 5th century. The significant differences in mound size and artifacts between Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun and the Ridge Cluster imply social stratification, with Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun representing a leader’s burial and the Ridge Cluster representing subordinate graves. Today, Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun is a Neyagawa City Designated Historic Site and is preserved within the Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun Park. File:Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Summit of the mound File:Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun, tsukuridashi-2.jpg|Tsukuridashi projection == Cultural Properties == === Neyagawa City Designated Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Properties ''' Deer-shaped Haniwa from Uzumasa Kofun Group (Archaeological Artifacts) - Stored at the Neyagawa City Buried Cultural Properties Museum. Designated on November 3, 2000[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html List of Designated Cultural Properties in Osaka Prefecture] (Osaka Prefecture website, updated November 21, 2018).. * Historic Sites ''' Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun - Designated on November 3, 1997. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Nishida Toshihide, "Kofun of Makimuku," in *Enjoyable Kofun Tour*, Shōraisha, pp. 84-86. * Ichimoto Yoshizo, "Uzumasa Kofun Group," in *Symposium: Understanding Osaka's Early Kofun*, Osaka Prefectural Yayoi Culture Museum, pp. 1-3, 2006. * *Uzumasa Kofun Group Excavation Results: Considering Osaka's Early Kofun Groups*, ed. Osaka Cultural Properties Center, 2006. == Related Topics == * {{ill|Nagahara Kofun Group|ja|長原古墳群}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun|Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun}} * [https://www.city.neyagawa.osaka.jp/organization_list/kyoiku_shakaikyoiku/bunkasport/bunkazai/namesagasu/1378193397977.html Uzumasa Kofun Group], [https://www.city.neyagawa.osaka.jp/Recommend/bunkazai/neyagawashishiteibunkazai/1382432802559.html Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun] - Neyagawa City Official Website [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:History of Neyagawa]] [[Category:kkk]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:kkk]] [[Category:Cluster burial mounds]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:kkk]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:kkk]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Uzumasa Kofun Cluster]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:original pages]][[category:Uzumasa Kofun Cluster]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Uzumasa Kofun Cluster]][[Category:pp]] [[ja:太秦古墳群]]

Designated on November 3, 2000[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html List of Designated Cultural Properties in Osaka Prefecture] (Osaka Prefecture website, updated November 21, 2018).. * Historic Sites ''' Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun - Designated on November 3, 1997. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Nishida Toshihide, "Kofun of Makimuku," in *Enjoyable Kofun Tour*, Shōraisha, pp. 84-86. * Ichimoto Yoshizo, "Uzumasa Kofun Group," in *Symposium: Understanding Osaka's Early Kofun*, Osaka Prefectural Yayoi Culture Museum, pp. 1-3, 2006. * *Uzumasa Kofun Group Excavation Results: Considering Osaka's Early Kofun Groups*, ed. Osaka Cultural Properties Center, 2006. == Related Topics == * {{ill|Nagahara Kofun Group|ja|長原古墳群}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun|Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun}} * [https://www.city.neyagawa.osaka.jp/organization_list/kyoiku_shakaikyoiku/bunkasport/bunkazai/namesagasu/1378193397977.html Uzumasa Kofun Group], [https://www.city.neyagawa.osaka.jp/Recommend/bunkazai/neyagawashishiteibunkazai/1382432802559.html Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun] - Neyagawa City Official Website [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:History of Neyagawa]] [[Category:kkk]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:kkk]] [[Category:Cluster burial mounds]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:kkk]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:kkk]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Uzumasa Kofun Cluster]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:original pages]][[category:Uzumasa Kofun Cluster]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Uzumasa Kofun Cluster]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Uzumasa Kofun Cluster]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[ja:太秦古墳群]]

{{Location map|Japan Osaka Prefecture|lat_deg=34|lat_min=45|lat_sec=28.60|lon_deg=135|lon_min=38|lon_sec=46.87|label=Uzumasa Kofun Group|position=top|width=220|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{center|Location of Uzumasa Kofun Group}}}} '''Uzumasa Kofun Group''' (太秦古墳群, Uzumasa Kofun-gun) refers to a cluster of ancient burial mounds (kofun) located on the eastern hills of {{ill|Neyagawa, Osaka|ja|寝屋川市}}, [[Osaka Prefecture]]. Presently, all tumuli except the Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun (a Neyagawa City Designated Historic Site) have been lost. == Overview == [[File:Uzumasa Kofun Group-18, funkyu.jpg|thumb|220px|right|Restored Mound of Kofun No. 18]] [[File:太秦古墳群 出土品.JPG|thumb|220px|right|Artifacts from the site
{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at the {{ill|Chikatsu Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}}.}}]] The Uzumasa Kofun Group once extended across the hills around Uzumasa Takatsuka Town and Kokumori Town. Besides the known Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun, fragments of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} have been unearthed from various locations in the area. The presence of place names such as Koganezuka, Mukaihizuka, Mawashizuka, Morozuka, Kaminoyama, Hakunoyama, Matsutanidaniyama, and Dandaniyama suggests a broad distribution of burial mounds in the past. Notably, in 1962, a deer-shaped haniwa was excavated from behind the Uzumasa Atsuta Shrine. In 1965, 300 meters south of Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun, the remains of a burial believed to involve direct wooden coffin interment were discovered, yielding iron arrowheads and straight swords. Additional artifacts such as triple-ring bells, gold rings, bronze arrowheads, comma-shaped beads, and spindle whorls have also been found. Collectively, these findings are referred to as the Uzumasa Kofun GroupNishida (1994), p. 85.. From 2001 to 2003, the {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Cultural Properties Center|ja|大阪府文化財センター}} conducted investigations in preparation for constructing the {{ill|Second Keihan Highway|ja|第二京阪道路}}, unearthing remains of 25 small burial mounds, mostly flattened, dating from the mid-5th to early 6th centuries. These mounds, located along a ridge, are referred to as the Uzumasa Kofun Group's Ridge Cluster. Additionally, Neyagawa City carried out excavations in 2001 for the preservation of Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun, fully uncovering its structure. === Ridge Cluster Investigations === Investigations for the {{ill|Second Keihan Highway|ja|第二京阪道路}} revealed numerous remains of mound ditches along a ridge. Most mounds were already damaged, with burial structures identified in only one (Kofun No. 3), which contained a direct wooden coffin burial. These mounds ranged in size from 4 to 14 meters in diameter or length. Artifacts such as Sue ware and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} were recovered from the surrounding ditches, while haniwa were found at only two sites (Kofun Nos. 8 and 17), yielding cylindrical and lotus-shaped haniwa. Based on these finds, the Ridge Cluster is dated to the mid-5th to early 6th centuriesIchimoto (2005), p. 1.. === Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun === [[File:Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|thumb|Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun]] Located 800 meters northwest of the Ridge Cluster, Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun is the only mound within the group that remains intact. Excavations in 2001 revealed a two-tiered, 37-meter-diameter circular mound with a northwestern {{ill|Tsukuridashi|ja|造り出し}} projection. The mound lacked stone coverings but had cylindrical haniwa rows on the terrace and Tsukuridashi. Figurative haniwa, including depictions of humans, houses, shields, roosters, and waterfowl, were also found. Artifacts such as armor, arrowheads, axes, and horse fittings were unearthed from the burial chamber at the mound's summit. These findings suggest the mound was constructed in the late 5th century. The significant differences in mound size and artifacts between Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun and the Ridge Cluster imply social stratification, with Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun representing a leader’s burial and the Ridge Cluster representing subordinate graves. Today, Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun is a Neyagawa City Designated Historic Site and is preserved within the Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun Park. File:Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Summit of the mound File:Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun, tsukuridashi-2.jpg|Tsukuridashi projection == Cultural Properties == === Neyagawa City Designated Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Properties ''' Deer-shaped Haniwa from Uzumasa Kofun Group (Archaeological Artifacts) - Stored at the Neyagawa City Buried Cultural Properties Museum. Designated on November 3, 2000[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html List of Designated Cultural Properties in Osaka Prefecture] (Osaka Prefecture website, updated November 21, 2018).. * Historic Sites ''' Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun - Designated on November 3, 1997. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Nishida Toshihide, "Kofun of Makimuku," in *Enjoyable Kofun Tour*, Shōraisha, pp. 84-86. * Ichimoto Yoshizo, "Uzumasa Kofun Group," in *Symposium: Understanding Osaka's Early Kofun*, Osaka Prefectural Yayoi Culture Museum, pp. 1-3, 2006. * *Uzumasa Kofun Group Excavation Results: Considering Osaka's Early Kofun Groups*, ed. Osaka Cultural Properties Center, 2006. == Related Topics == * {{ill|Nagahara Kofun Group|ja|長原古墳群}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun|Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun}} * [https://www.city.neyagawa.osaka.jp/organization_list/kyoiku_shakaikyoiku/bunkasport/bunkazai/namesagasu/1378193397977.html Uzumasa Kofun Group], [https://www.city.neyagawa.osaka.jp/Recommend/bunkazai/neyagawashishiteibunkazai/1382432802559.html Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun] - Neyagawa City Official Website [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:History of Neyagawa]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:Cluster burial mounds]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Uzumasa Kofun Cluster]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:original pages]][[category:Uzumasa Kofun Cluster]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Uzumasa Kofun Cluster]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Uzumasa Kofun Cluster]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[ja:太秦古墳群]]
