Besides the known Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun, fragments of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/haniwa|qq=}} and {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sue pottery|13=User:Immanuelle/Sue pottery|qq=}} have been unearthed from various locations in the area. The presence of place names such as Koganezuka, Mukaihizuka, Mawashizuka, Morozuka, Kaminoyama, Hakunoyama, Matsutanidaniyama, and Dandaniyama suggests a broad distribution of Kofuns in the past. Notably, in 1962, a deer-shaped haniwa was excavated from behind the Uzumasa Atsuta Shrine. In 1965, 300 meters south of Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun, the remains of a burial believed to involve direct wooden coffin interment were discovered, yielding iron arrowheads and straight swords. Additional artifacts such as triple-ring bells, gold rings, bronze arrowheads, comma-shaped beads, and spindle whorls have also been found. Collectively, these findings are referred to as the Uzumasa Kofun GroupNishida (1994), p. 85.. From 2001 to 2003, the {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Cultural Properties Center|ja|大阪府文化財センター|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Prefectural Cultural Properties Center|qq=}} conducted investigations in preparation for constructing the {{ill|Second Keihan Highway|ja|第二京阪道路|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Second Keihan Highway|qq=}}, unearthing remains of 25 small Kofuns, mostly flattened, dating from the mid-5th to early 6th centuries. These mounds, located along a ridge, are referred to as the Uzumasa Kofun Group's Ridge Cluster. Additionally, Neyagawa City carried out excavations in 2001 for the preservation of Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun, fully uncovering its structure. === Ridge Cluster Investigations === Investigations for the {{ill|Second Keihan Highway|ja|第二京阪道路|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Second Keihan Highway|qq=}} revealed numerous remains of mound ditches along a ridge. Most mounds were already damaged, with burial structures identified in only one (Kofun No. 3), which contained a direct wooden coffin burial. These mounds ranged in size from 4 to 14 meters in diameter or length. Artifacts such as Sue pottery and {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haji pottery|13=User:Immanuelle/Haji pottery|qq=}} were recovered from the surrounding ditches, while haniwa were found at only two sites (Kofun Nos. 8 and 17), yielding cylindrical and lotus-shaped haniwa. Based on these finds, the Ridge Cluster is dated to the mid-5th to early 6th centuriesIchimoto (2005), p. 1.. === Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun === [[File:Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|thumb|Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun]] Located 800 meters northwest of the Ridge Cluster, Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun is the only mound within the group that remains intact. Excavations in 2001 revealed a two-tiered, 37-meter-diameter circular mound with a northwestern {{ill|Tsukuridashi|ja|造り出し|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukuridashi|qq=}} projection. The mound lacked stone coverings but had cylindrical haniwa rows on the terrace and Tsukuridashi. Figurative haniwa, including depictions of humans, houses, shields, roosters, and waterfowl, were also found. Artifacts such as armor, arrowheads, axes, and horse fittings were unearthed from the burial chamber at the mound's summit. These findings suggest the mound was constructed in the late 5th century. The significant differences in mound size and artifacts between Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun and the Ridge Cluster imply social stratification, with Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun representing a leader’s burial and the Ridge Cluster representing subordinate graves. Today, Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun is a Neyagawa City Designated Historic Site and is preserved within the Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun Park. File:Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Summit of the mound File:Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun, tsukuridashi-2.jpg|Tsukuridashi projection == Cultural Properties == === Neyagawa City Designated Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Properties ''' Deer-shaped Haniwa from Uzumasa Kofun Group (Archaeological Artifacts) - Stored at the Neyagawa City Buried Cultural Properties Museum. Designated on November 3, 2000[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html List of Designated Cultural Properties in Osaka Prefecture] (Osaka Prefecture website, updated November 21, 2018).. * Historic Sites ''' Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun - Designated on November 3, 1997. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Nishida Toshihide, "Kofun of Makimuku," in *Enjoyable Kofun Tour*, Shōraisha, 11. 84-86. * Ichimoto Yoshizo, "Uzumasa Kofun Group," in *Symposium: Understanding Osaka's Early Kofun*, Osaka Prefectural Yayoi Culture Museum, 11. 1-3, 2006. * *Uzumasa Kofun Group Excavation Results: Considering Osaka's Early Kofun Groups*, ed. Osaka Cultural Properties Center, 2006. == See Also == * {{ill|Nagahara Kofun Cluster|lt=Nagahara Kofun Cluster|lt=Nagahara Kofun Group|ja|長原古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagahara Kofun Group|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun|Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun}} * [https://www.city.neyagawa.osaka.jp/organization_list/kyoiku_shakaikyoiku/bunkasport/bunkazai/namesagasu/1378193397977.html Uzumasa Kofun Group], [https://www.city.neyagawa.osaka.jp/Recommend/bunkazai/neyagawashishiteibunkazai/1382432802559.html Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun] - Neyagawa City Official Website {{translated page|ja|太秦古墳群|version=81546838|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:History of Neyagawa]] [[Category:Cluster Kofuns]] }} [[en:Uzumasa Kofun Cluster]] [[sn:Uzumasa Kofun Cluster]] [[ja:太秦古墳群]] [[sn:zumasa Kofun Cluster]]

revision.comment: Bot: add category Category:寝屋川市の歴史
revision.text:
{{Location map|Japan Osaka Prefecture|lat_deg=34|lat_min=45|lat_sec=28.60|lon_deg=135|lon_min=38|lon_sec=46.87|label=Uzumasa Kofun Group|position=top|width=220|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{center|Location of Uzumasa Kofun Group}}}} '''Uzumasa Kofun Group''' (太秦古墳群, Uzumasa Kofun-gun) refers to a cluster of ancient Kofuns (kofun) located on the eastern hills of [[:en:Neyagawa, Osaka|Neyagawa, Osaka]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]. Presently, all tumuli except the Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun (a Neyagawa City Designated Historic Site) have been lost. == Overview == [[File:Uzumasa Kofun Group-18, funkyu.jpg|thumb|220px|right|Restored Mound of Kofun No. 18]] [[File:太秦古墳群 出土品.JPG|thumb|220px|right|burial goods from the site
{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]].}]]] The Uzumasa Kofun Group once extended across the hills around Uzumasa Takatsuka Town and Kokumori Town. Besides the known Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun, fragments of [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] and [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] have been unearthed from various locations in the area. The presence of place names such as Koganezuka, Mukaihizuka, Mawashizuka, Morozuka, Kaminoyama, Hakunoyama, Matsutanidaniyama, and Dandaniyama suggests a broad distribution of Kofuns in the past. Notably, in 1962, a deer-shaped haniwa was excavated from behind the Uzumasa Atsuta Shrine. In 1965, 300 meters south of Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun, the remains of a burial believed to involve direct wooden coffin interment were discovered, yielding iron arrowheads and straight swords. Additional artifacts such as triple-ring bells, gold rings, bronze arrowheads, comma-shaped beads, and spindle whorls have also been found. Collectively, these findings are referred to as the Uzumasa Kofun GroupNishida (1994), p. 85.. From 2001 to 2003, the {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Cultural Properties Center|ja|大阪府文化財センター|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Prefectural Cultural Properties Center|qq=}} conducted investigations in preparation for constructing the [[:en:Second Keihan Highway|Second Keihan Highway]], unearthing remains of 25 small Kofuns, mostly flattened, dating from the mid-5th to early 6th centuries. These mounds, located along a ridge, are referred to as the Uzumasa Kofun Group's Ridge Cluster. Additionally, Neyagawa City carried out excavations in 2001 for the preservation of Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun, fully uncovering its structure. === Ridge Cluster Investigations === Investigations for the [[:en:Second Keihan Highway|Second Keihan Highway]] revealed numerous remains of mound ditches along a ridge. Most mounds were already damaged, with burial structures identified in only one (Kofun No. 3), which contained a direct wooden coffin burial. These mounds ranged in size from 4 to 14 meters in diameter or length. Artifacts such as Sue pottery and [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] were recovered from the surrounding ditches, while haniwa were found at only two sites (Kofun Nos. 8 and 17), yielding cylindrical and lotus-shaped haniwa. Based on these finds, the Ridge Cluster is dated to the mid-5th to early 6th centuriesIchimoto (2005), p. 1.. === Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun === [[File:Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|thumb|Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun]] Located 800 meters northwest of the Ridge Cluster, Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun is the only mound within the group that remains intact. Excavations in 2001 revealed a two-tiered, 37-meter-diameter circular mound with a northwestern {{ill|Platform-style projection|lt=Platform-style projection|lt=Tsukuridashi|ja|造り出し|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukuridashi|qq=}} projection. The mound lacked stone coverings but had cylindrical haniwa rows on the terrace and Tsukuridashi. Figurative haniwa, including depictions of humans, houses, shields, roosters, and waterfowl, were also found. Artifacts such as armor, arrowheads, axes, and horse fittings were unearthed from the burial chamber at the mound's summit. These findings suggest the mound was constructed in the late 5th century. The significant differences in mound size and artifacts between Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun and the Ridge Cluster imply social stratification, with Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun representing a leader’s burial and the Ridge Cluster representing subordinate graves. Today, Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun is a Neyagawa City Designated Historic Site and is preserved within the Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun Park. File:Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Summit of the mound File:Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun, tsukuridashi-2.jpg|Tsukuridashi projection == Cultural Properties == === Neyagawa City Designated Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Properties ''' Deer-shaped Haniwa from Uzumasa Kofun Group (Archaeological Artifacts) - Stored at the Neyagawa City Buried Cultural Properties Museum. Designated on November 3, 2000[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html List of Designated Cultural Properties in Osaka Prefecture] (Osaka Prefecture website, updated November 21, 2018).. * Historic Sites ''' Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun - Designated on November 3, 1997. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Nishida Toshihide, "Kofun of Makimuku," in *Enjoyable Kofun Tour*, Shōraisha, 11. 84-86. * Ichimoto Yoshizo, "Uzumasa Kofun Group," in *Symposium: Understanding Osaka's Early Kofun*, Osaka Prefectural Yayoi Culture Museum, 11. 1-3, 2006. * *Uzumasa Kofun Group Excavation Results: Considering Osaka's Early Kofun Groups*, ed. Osaka Cultural Properties Center, 2006. == See Also == * {{ill|Nagahara Kofun Cluster|lt=Nagahara Kofun Cluster|lt=Nagahara Kofun Group|ja|長原古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagahara Kofun Group|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun|Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun}} * [https://www.city.neyagawa.osaka.jp/organization_list/kyoiku_shakaikyoiku/bunkasport/bunkazai/namesagasu/1378193397977.html Uzumasa Kofun Group], [https://www.city.neyagawa.osaka.jp/Recommend/bunkazai/neyagawashishiteibunkazai/1382432802559.html Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun] - Neyagawa City Official Website {{translated page|ja|太秦古墳群|version=81546838|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:History of Neyagawa]] [[Category:Cluster Kofuns]] }} [[en:Uzumasa Kofun Cluster]] [[sn:Uzumasa Kofun Cluster]] [[ja:太秦古墳群]] [[sn:zumasa Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:大阪府の古墳]] [[Category:寝屋川市の歴史]]

Figurative haniwa, including depictions of humans, houses, shields, roosters, and waterfowl, were also found. Artifacts such as armor, arrowheads, axes, and horse fittings were unearthed from the burial chamber at the mound's summit. These findings suggest the mound was constructed in the late 5th century. The significant differences in mound size and artifacts between Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun and the Ridge Cluster imply social stratification, with Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun representing a leader’s burial and the Ridge Cluster representing subordinate graves. Today, Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun is a Neyagawa City Designated Historic Site and is preserved within the Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun Park. File:Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Summit of the mound File:Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun, tsukuridashi-2.jpg|Tsukuridashi projection == Cultural Properties == === Neyagawa City Designated Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Properties ''' Deer-shaped Haniwa from Uzumasa Kofun Group (Archaeological Artifacts) - Stored at the Neyagawa City Buried Cultural Properties Museum. Designated on November 3, 2000[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html List of Designated Cultural Properties in Osaka Prefecture] (Osaka Prefecture website, updated November 21, 2018).. * Historic Sites ''' Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun - Designated on November 3, 1997. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Nishida Toshihide, "Kofun of Makimuku," in *Enjoyable Kofun Tour*, Shōraisha, 11. 84-86. * Ichimoto Yoshizo, "Uzumasa Kofun Group," in *Symposium: Understanding Osaka's Early Kofun*, Osaka Prefectural Yayoi Culture Museum, 11. 1-3, 2006. * *Uzumasa Kofun Group Excavation Results: Considering Osaka's Early Kofun Groups*, ed. revision.comment: Bot: remove invalid enwiki interwiki links
revision.text:
{{Location map|Japan Osaka Prefecture|lat_deg=34|lat_min=45|lat_sec=28.60|lon_deg=135|lon_min=38|lon_sec=46.87|label=Uzumasa Kofun Group|position=top|width=220|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{center|Location of Uzumasa Kofun Group}}}} '''Uzumasa Kofun Group''' (太秦古墳群, Uzumasa Kofun-gun) refers to a cluster of ancient Kofuns (kofun) located on the eastern hills of [[:en:Neyagawa, Osaka|Neyagawa, Osaka]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]. Presently, all tumuli except the Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun (a Neyagawa City Designated Historic Site) have been lost. == Overview == [[File:Uzumasa Kofun Group-18, funkyu.jpg|thumb|220px|right|Restored Mound of Kofun No. 18]] [[File:太秦古墳群 出土品.JPG|thumb|220px|right|burial goods from the site
{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]].}]]] The Uzumasa Kofun Group once extended across the hills around Uzumasa Takatsuka Town and Kokumori Town. Besides the known Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun, fragments of [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] and [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] have been unearthed from various locations in the area. The presence of place names such as Koganezuka, Mukaihizuka, Mawashizuka, Morozuka, Kaminoyama, Hakunoyama, Matsutanidaniyama, and Dandaniyama suggests a broad distribution of Kofuns in the past. Notably, in 1962, a deer-shaped haniwa was excavated from behind the Uzumasa Atsuta Shrine. In 1965, 300 meters south of Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun, the remains of a burial believed to involve direct wooden coffin interment were discovered, yielding iron arrowheads and straight swords. Additional artifacts such as triple-ring bells, gold rings, bronze arrowheads, comma-shaped beads, and spindle whorls have also been found. Collectively, these findings are referred to as the Uzumasa Kofun GroupNishida (1994), p. 85.. From 2001 to 2003, the {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Cultural Properties Center|ja|大阪府文化財センター|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Prefectural Cultural Properties Center|qq=}} conducted investigations in preparation for constructing the [[:en:Second Keihan Highway|Second Keihan Highway]], unearthing remains of 25 small Kofuns, mostly flattened, dating from the mid-5th to early 6th centuries. These mounds, located along a ridge, are referred to as the Uzumasa Kofun Group's Ridge Cluster. Additionally, Neyagawa City carried out excavations in 2001 for the preservation of Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun, fully uncovering its structure. === Ridge Cluster Investigations === Investigations for the [[:en:Second Keihan Highway|Second Keihan Highway]] revealed numerous remains of mound ditches along a ridge. Most mounds were already damaged, with burial structures identified in only one (Kofun No. 3), which contained a direct wooden coffin burial. These mounds ranged in size from 4 to 14 meters in diameter or length. Artifacts such as Sue pottery and [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] were recovered from the surrounding ditches, while haniwa were found at only two sites (Kofun Nos. 8 and 17), yielding cylindrical and lotus-shaped haniwa. Based on these finds, the Ridge Cluster is dated to the mid-5th to early 6th centuriesIchimoto (2005), p. 1.. === Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun === [[File:Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|thumb|Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun]] Located 800 meters northwest of the Ridge Cluster, Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun is the only mound within the group that remains intact. Excavations in 2001 revealed a two-tiered, 37-meter-diameter circular mound with a northwestern {{ill|Platform-style projection|lt=Platform-style projection|lt=Tsukuridashi|ja|造り出し|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukuridashi|qq=}} projection. The mound lacked stone coverings but had cylindrical haniwa rows on the terrace and Tsukuridashi. Figurative haniwa, including depictions of humans, houses, shields, roosters, and waterfowl, were also found. Artifacts such as armor, arrowheads, axes, and horse fittings were unearthed from the burial chamber at the mound's summit. These findings suggest the mound was constructed in the late 5th century. The significant differences in mound size and artifacts between Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun and the Ridge Cluster imply social stratification, with Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun representing a leader’s burial and the Ridge Cluster representing subordinate graves. Today, Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun is a Neyagawa City Designated Historic Site and is preserved within the Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun Park. File:Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Summit of the mound File:Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun, tsukuridashi-2.jpg|Tsukuridashi projection == Cultural Properties == === Neyagawa City Designated Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Properties ''' Deer-shaped Haniwa from Uzumasa Kofun Group (Archaeological Artifacts) - Stored at the Neyagawa City Buried Cultural Properties Museum. Designated on November 3, 2000[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html List of Designated Cultural Properties in Osaka Prefecture] (Osaka Prefecture website, updated November 21, 2018).. * Historic Sites ''' Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun - Designated on November 3, 1997. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Nishida Toshihide, "Kofun of Makimuku," in *Enjoyable Kofun Tour*, Shōraisha, 11. 84-86. * Ichimoto Yoshizo, "Uzumasa Kofun Group," in *Symposium: Understanding Osaka's Early Kofun*, Osaka Prefectural Yayoi Culture Museum, 11. 1-3, 2006. * *Uzumasa Kofun Group Excavation Results: Considering Osaka's Early Kofun Groups*, ed. Osaka Cultural Properties Center, 2006. == See Also == * {{ill|Nagahara Kofun Cluster|lt=Nagahara Kofun Cluster|lt=Nagahara Kofun Group|ja|長原古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagahara Kofun Group|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun|Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun}} * [https://www.city.neyagawa.osaka.jp/organization_list/kyoiku_shakaikyoiku/bunkasport/bunkazai/namesagasu/1378193397977.html Uzumasa Kofun Group], [https://www.city.neyagawa.osaka.jp/Recommend/bunkazai/neyagawashishiteibunkazai/1382432802559.html Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun] - Neyagawa City Official Website {{translated page|ja|太秦古墳群|version=81546838|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:History of Neyagawa]] [[Category:Cluster Kofuns]] }} [[sn:Uzumasa Kofun Cluster]] [[ja:太秦古墳群]] [[sn:zumasa Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:大阪府の古墳]] [[Category:History of Neyagawa, Osaka]] [[Category:群集墳]]

Artifacts such as Sue pottery and [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] were recovered from the surrounding ditches, while haniwa were found at only two sites (Kofun Nos. 8 and 17), yielding cylindrical and lotus-shaped haniwa. Based on these finds, the Ridge Cluster is dated to the mid-5th to early 6th centuriesIchimoto (2005), p. 1.. === Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun === [[File:Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|thumb|Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun]] Located 800 meters northwest of the Ridge Cluster, Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun is the only mound within the group that remains intact. Excavations in 2001 revealed a two-tiered, 37-meter-diameter circular mound with a northwestern {{ill|Platform-style projection|lt=Platform-style projection|lt=Tsukuridashi|ja|造り出し|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukuridashi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 造出}} projection. The mound lacked stone coverings but had cylindrical haniwa rows on the terrace and Tsukuridashi. Figurative haniwa, including depictions of humans, houses, shields, roosters, and waterfowl, were also found. Artifacts such as armor, arrowheads, axes, and horse fittings were unearthed from the burial chamber at the mound's summit. These findings suggest the mound was constructed in the late 5th century. The significant differences in mound size and artifacts between Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun and the Ridge Cluster imply social stratification, with Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun representing a leader’s burial and the Ridge Cluster representing subordinate graves. Today, Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun is a Neyagawa City Designated Historic Site and is preserved within the Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun Park. File:Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Summit of the mound File:Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun, tsukuridashi-2.jpg|Tsukuridashi projection == Cultural Properties == === Neyagawa City Designated Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Properties ''' Deer-shaped Haniwa from Uzumasa Kofun Group (Archaeological Artifacts) - Stored at the Neyagawa City Buried Cultural Properties Museum. Designated on November 3, 2000[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html List of Designated Cultural Properties in Osaka Prefecture] (Osaka Prefecture website, updated November 21, 2018).. * Historic Sites ''' Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun - Designated on November 3, 1997. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Nishida Toshihide, "Kofun of Makimuku," in *Enjoyable Kofun Tour*, Shōraisha, 11. 84-86. * Ichimoto Yoshizo, "Uzumasa Kofun Group," in *Symposium: Understanding Osaka's Early Kofun*, Osaka Prefectural Yayoi Culture Museum, 11. 1-3, 2006. * *Uzumasa Kofun Group Excavation Results: Considering Osaka's Early Kofun Groups*, ed. Osaka Cultural Properties Center, 2006. == See Also == * {{ill|Nagahara Kofun Cluster|lt=Nagahara Kofun Cluster|lt=Nagahara Kofun Group|ja|長原古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagahara Kofun Group|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun|Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun}} * [https://www.city.neyagawa.osaka.jp/organization_list/kyoiku_shakaikyoiku/bunkasport/bunkazai/namesagasu/1378193397977.html Uzumasa Kofun Group], [https://www.city.neyagawa.osaka.jp/Recommend/bunkazai/neyagawashishiteibunkazai/1382432802559.html Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun] - Neyagawa City Official Website {{translated page|ja|太秦古墳群|version=81546838|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:History of Neyagawa]] [[Category:Cluster Kofuns]] }} [[sn:Uzumasa Kofun Cluster]] [[ja:太秦古墳群]] [[sn:zumasa Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:History of Neyagawa, Osaka]] [[Category:群集墳]]

revision.comment: Bot: remove deleted category Cluster Kofuns
revision.text:
{{Location map|Japan Osaka Prefecture|lat_deg=34|lat_min=45|lat_sec=28.60|lon_deg=135|lon_min=38|lon_sec=46.87|label=Uzumasa Kofun Group|position=top|width=220|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{center|Location of Uzumasa Kofun Group}}}} '''Uzumasa Kofun Group''' (太秦古墳群, Uzumasa Kofun-gun) refers to a cluster of ancient Kofuns (kofun) located on the eastern hills of [[:en:Neyagawa, Osaka|Neyagawa, Osaka]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]. Presently, all tumuli except the Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun (a Neyagawa City Designated Historic Site) have been lost. == Overview == [[File:Uzumasa Kofun Group-18, funkyu.jpg|thumb|220px|right|Restored Mound of Kofun No. 18]] [[File:太秦古墳群 出土品.JPG|thumb|220px|right|burial goods from the site
{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]].}]]] The Uzumasa Kofun Group once extended across the hills around Uzumasa Takatsuka Town and Kokumori Town. Besides the known Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun, fragments of [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] and [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] have been unearthed from various locations in the area. The presence of place names such as Koganezuka, Mukaihizuka, Mawashizuka, Morozuka, Kaminoyama, Hakunoyama, Matsutanidaniyama, and Dandaniyama suggests a broad distribution of Kofuns in the past. Notably, in 1962, a deer-shaped haniwa was excavated from behind the Uzumasa Atsuta Shrine. In 1965, 300 meters south of Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun, the remains of a burial believed to involve direct wooden coffin interment were discovered, yielding iron arrowheads and straight swords. Additional artifacts such as triple-ring bells, gold rings, bronze arrowheads, comma-shaped beads, and spindle whorls have also been found. Collectively, these findings are referred to as the Uzumasa Kofun GroupNishida (1994), p. 85.. From 2001 to 2003, the {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Cultural Properties Center|ja|大阪府文化財センター|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Prefectural Cultural Properties Center|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} conducted investigations in preparation for constructing the [[:en:Second Keihan Highway|Second Keihan Highway]], unearthing remains of 25 small Kofuns, mostly flattened, dating from the mid-5th to early 6th centuries. These mounds, located along a ridge, are referred to as the Uzumasa Kofun Group's Ridge Cluster. Additionally, Neyagawa City carried out excavations in 2001 for the preservation of Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun, fully uncovering its structure. === Ridge Cluster Investigations === Investigations for the [[:en:Second Keihan Highway|Second Keihan Highway]] revealed numerous remains of mound ditches along a ridge. Most mounds were already damaged, with burial structures identified in only one (Kofun No. 3), which contained a direct wooden coffin burial. These mounds ranged in size from 4 to 14 meters in diameter or length. Artifacts such as Sue pottery and [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] were recovered from the surrounding ditches, while haniwa were found at only two sites (Kofun Nos. 8 and 17), yielding cylindrical and lotus-shaped haniwa. Based on these finds, the Ridge Cluster is dated to the mid-5th to early 6th centuriesIchimoto (2005), p. 1.. === Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun === [[File:Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|thumb|Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun]] Located 800 meters northwest of the Ridge Cluster, Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun is the only mound within the group that remains intact. Excavations in 2001 revealed a two-tiered, 37-meter-diameter circular mound with a northwestern {{ill|Platform-style projection|lt=Platform-style projection|lt=Tsukuridashi|ja|造り出し|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukuridashi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 造出}} projection. The mound lacked stone coverings but had cylindrical haniwa rows on the terrace and Tsukuridashi. Figurative haniwa, including depictions of humans, houses, shields, roosters, and waterfowl, were also found. Artifacts such as armor, arrowheads, axes, and horse fittings were unearthed from the burial chamber at the mound's summit. These findings suggest the mound was constructed in the late 5th century. The significant differences in mound size and artifacts between Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun and the Ridge Cluster imply social stratification, with Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun representing a leader’s burial and the Ridge Cluster representing subordinate graves. Today, Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun is a Neyagawa City Designated Historic Site and is preserved within the Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun Park. File:Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Summit of the mound File:Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun, tsukuridashi-2.jpg|Tsukuridashi projection == Cultural Properties == === Neyagawa City Designated Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Properties ''' Deer-shaped Haniwa from Uzumasa Kofun Group (Archaeological Artifacts) - Stored at the Neyagawa City Buried Cultural Properties Museum. Designated on November 3, 2000[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html List of Designated Cultural Properties in Osaka Prefecture] (Osaka Prefecture website, updated November 21, 2018).. * Historic Sites ''' Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun - Designated on November 3, 1997. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Nishida Toshihide, "Kofun of Makimuku," in *Enjoyable Kofun Tour*, Shōraisha, 11. 84-86. * Ichimoto Yoshizo, "Uzumasa Kofun Group," in *Symposium: Understanding Osaka's Early Kofun*, Osaka Prefectural Yayoi Culture Museum, 11. 1-3, 2006. * *Uzumasa Kofun Group Excavation Results: Considering Osaka's Early Kofun Groups*, ed. Osaka Cultural Properties Center, 2006. == See Also == * {{ill|Nagahara Kofun Cluster|lt=Nagahara Kofun Cluster|lt=Nagahara Kofun Group|ja|長原古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagahara Kofun Group|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun|Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun}} * [https://www.city.neyagawa.osaka.jp/organization_list/kyoiku_shakaikyoiku/bunkasport/bunkazai/namesagasu/1378193397977.html Uzumasa Kofun Group], [https://www.city.neyagawa.osaka.jp/Recommend/bunkazai/neyagawashishiteibunkazai/1382432802559.html Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun] - Neyagawa City Official Website {{translated page|ja|太秦古墳群|version=81546838|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:History of Neyagawa]] }} [[sn:Uzumasa Kofun Cluster]] [[ja:太秦古墳群]] [[sn:zumasa Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:History of Neyagawa, Osaka]] [[Category:群集墳]]

From 2001 to 2003, the {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Cultural Properties Center|ja|大阪府文化財センター|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Prefectural Cultural Properties Center|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} conducted investigations in preparation for constructing the [[:en:Second Keihan Highway|Second Keihan Highway]], unearthing remains of 25 small Kofuns, mostly flattened, dating from the mid-5th to early 6th centuries. These mounds, located along a ridge, are referred to as the Uzumasa Kofun Group's Ridge Cluster. Additionally, Neyagawa City carried out excavations in 2001 for the preservation of Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun, fully uncovering its structure. === Ridge Cluster Investigations === Investigations for the [[:en:Second Keihan Highway|Second Keihan Highway]] revealed numerous remains of mound ditches along a ridge. Most mounds were already damaged, with burial structures identified in only one (Kofun No. 3), which contained a direct wooden coffin burial. These mounds ranged in size from 4 to 14 meters in diameter or length. Artifacts such as Sue pottery and [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] were recovered from the surrounding ditches, while haniwa were found at only two sites (Kofun Nos. 8 and 17), yielding cylindrical and lotus-shaped haniwa. Based on these finds, the Ridge Cluster is dated to the mid-5th to early 6th centuriesIchimoto (2005), p. 1.. === Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun === [[File:Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|thumb|Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun]] Located 800 meters northwest of the Ridge Cluster, Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun is the only mound within the group that remains intact. Excavations in 2001 revealed a two-tiered, 37-meter-diameter circular mound with a northwestern {{ill|Platform-style projection|lt=Platform-style projection|lt=Tsukuridashi|ja|造り出し|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukuridashi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 造出}} projection. The mound lacked stone coverings but had cylindrical haniwa rows on the terrace and Tsukuridashi. Figurative haniwa, including depictions of humans, houses, shields, roosters, and waterfowl, were also found. Artifacts such as armor, arrowheads, axes, and horse fittings were unearthed from the burial chamber at the mound's summit. These findings suggest the mound was constructed in the late 5th century. The significant differences in mound size and artifacts between Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun and the Ridge Cluster imply social stratification, with Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun representing a leader’s burial and the Ridge Cluster representing subordinate graves. Today, Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun is a Neyagawa City Designated Historic Site and is preserved within the Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun Park. File:Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Summit of the mound File:Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun, tsukuridashi-2.jpg|Tsukuridashi projection == Cultural Properties == === Neyagawa City Designated Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Properties ''' Deer-shaped Haniwa from Uzumasa Kofun Group (Archaeological Artifacts) - Stored at the Neyagawa City Buried Cultural Properties Museum. Designated on November 3, 2000[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html List of Designated Cultural Properties in Osaka Prefecture] (Osaka Prefecture website, updated November 21, 2018).. * Historic Sites ''' Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun - Designated on November 3, 1997. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Nishida Toshihide, "Kofun of Makimuku," in *Enjoyable Kofun Tour*, Shōraisha, 11. 84-86. * Ichimoto Yoshizo, "Uzumasa Kofun Group," in *Symposium: Understanding Osaka's Early Kofun*, Osaka Prefectural Yayoi Culture Museum, 11. 1-3, 2006. * *Uzumasa Kofun Group Excavation Results: Considering Osaka's Early Kofun Groups*, ed. Osaka Cultural Properties Center, 2006. == See Also == * {{ill|Nagahara Kofun Cluster|lt=Nagahara Kofun Cluster|lt=Nagahara Kofun Group|ja|長原古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagahara Kofun Group|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun|Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun}} * [https://www.city.neyagawa.osaka.jp/organization_list/kyoiku_shakaikyoiku/bunkasport/bunkazai/namesagasu/1378193397977.html Uzumasa Kofun Group], [https://www.city.neyagawa.osaka.jp/Recommend/bunkazai/neyagawashishiteibunkazai/1382432802559.html Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun] - Neyagawa City Official Website {{translated page|ja|太秦古墳群|version=81546838|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[sn:Uzumasa Kofun Cluster]] [[ja:太秦古墳群]] [[sn:zumasa Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Gunshūfun]]

revision.comment: Bot: prepend Wikidata AFC comment via pages.txt
revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11444484]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Location map|Japan Osaka Prefecture|lat_deg=34|lat_min=45|lat_sec=28.60|lon_deg=135|lon_min=38|lon_sec=46.87|label=Uzumasa Kofun Group|position=top|width=220|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{center|Location of Uzumasa Kofun Group}}}} '''Uzumasa Kofun Group''' (太秦古墳群, Uzumasa Kofun-gun) refers to a cluster of ancient Kofuns (kofun) located on the eastern hills of [[:en:Neyagawa, Osaka|Neyagawa, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]]. Presently, all tumuli except the Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun (a Neyagawa City Designated Historic Site) have been lost. == Overview == [[File:Uzumasa Kofun Group-18, funkyu.jpg|thumb|220px|right|Restored Mound of Kofun No. 18]] [[File:太秦古墳群 出土品.JPG|thumb|220px|right|burial goods from the site
{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]].}]]] The Uzumasa Kofun Group once extended across the hills around Uzumasa Takatsuka Town and Kokumori Town. Besides the known Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun, fragments of [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] and [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] have been unearthed from various locations in the area. The presence of place names such as Koganezuka, Mukaihizuka, Mawashizuka, Morozuka, Kaminoyama, Hakunoyama, Matsutanidaniyama, and Dandaniyama suggests a broad distribution of Kofuns in the past. Notably, in 1962, a deer-shaped haniwa was excavated from behind the Uzumasa Atsuta Shrine. In 1965, 300 meters south of Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun, the remains of a burial believed to involve direct wooden coffin interment were discovered, yielding iron arrowheads and straight swords. Additional artifacts such as triple-ring bells, gold rings, bronze arrowheads, comma-shaped beads, and spindle whorls have also been found. Collectively, these findings are referred to as the Uzumasa Kofun GroupNishida (1994), p. 85.. From 2001 to 2003, the {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Cultural Properties Center|ja|大阪府文化財センター|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Prefectural Cultural Properties Center|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} conducted investigations in preparation for constructing the [[:en:Second Keihan Highway|Second Keihan Highway]], unearthing remains of 25 small Kofuns, mostly flattened, dating from the mid-5th to early 6th centuries. These mounds, located along a ridge, are referred to as the Uzumasa Kofun Group's Ridge Cluster. Additionally, Neyagawa City carried out excavations in 2001 for the preservation of Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun, fully uncovering its structure. === Ridge Cluster Investigations === Investigations for the [[:en:Second Keihan Highway|Second Keihan Highway]] revealed numerous remains of mound ditches along a ridge. Most mounds were already damaged, with burial structures identified in only one (Kofun No. 3), which contained a direct wooden coffin burial. These mounds ranged in size from 4 to 14 meters in diameter or length. Artifacts such as Sue pottery and [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] were recovered from the surrounding ditches, while haniwa were found at only two sites (Kofun Nos. 8 and 17), yielding cylindrical and lotus-shaped haniwa. Based on these finds, the Ridge Cluster is dated to the mid-5th to early 6th centuriesIchimoto (2005), p. 1.. === Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun === [[File:Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|thumb|Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun]] Located 800 meters northwest of the Ridge Cluster, Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun is the only mound within the group that remains intact. Excavations in 2001 revealed a two-tiered, 37-meter-diameter circular mound with a northwestern {{ill|Platform-style projection|lt=Platform-style projection|lt=Tsukuridashi|ja|造り出し|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukuridashi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 造出}} projection. The mound lacked stone coverings but had cylindrical haniwa rows on the terrace and Tsukuridashi. Figurative haniwa, including depictions of humans, houses, shields, roosters, and waterfowl, were also found. Artifacts such as armor, arrowheads, axes, and horse fittings were unearthed from the burial chamber at the mound's summit. These findings suggest the mound was constructed in the late 5th century. The significant differences in mound size and artifacts between Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun and the Ridge Cluster imply social stratification, with Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun representing a leader’s burial and the Ridge Cluster representing subordinate graves. Today, Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun is a Neyagawa City Designated Historic Site and is preserved within the Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun Park. File:Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Summit of the mound File:Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun, tsukuridashi-2.jpg|Tsukuridashi projection == Cultural Properties == === Neyagawa City Designated Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Properties ''' Deer-shaped Haniwa from Uzumasa Kofun Group (Archaeological Artifacts) - Stored at the Neyagawa City Buried Cultural Properties Museum. Designated on November 3, 2000[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html List of Designated Cultural Properties in Osaka Prefecture] (Osaka Prefecture website, updated November 21, 2018).. * Historic Sites ''' Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun - Designated on November 3, 1997. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Nishida Toshihide, "Kofun of Makimuku," in *Enjoyable Kofun Tour*, Shōraisha, 11. 84-86. * Ichimoto Yoshizo, "Uzumasa Kofun Group," in *Symposium: Understanding Osaka's Early Kofun*, Osaka Prefectural Yayoi Culture Museum, 11. 1-3, 2006. * *Uzumasa Kofun Group Excavation Results: Considering Osaka's Early Kofun Groups*, ed. Osaka Cultural Properties Center, 2006. == See Also == * {{ill|Nagahara Kofun Cluster|lt=Nagahara Kofun Cluster|lt=Nagahara Kofun Group|ja|長原古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagahara Kofun Group|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun|Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun}} * [https://www.city.neyagawa.osaka.jp/organization_list/kyoiku_shakaikyoiku/bunkasport/bunkazai/namesagasu/1378193397977.html Uzumasa Kofun Group], [https://www.city.neyagawa.osaka.jp/Recommend/bunkazai/neyagawashishiteibunkazai/1382432802559.html Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun] - Neyagawa City Official Website {{translated page|ja|太秦古墳群|version=81546838|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[sn:Uzumasa Kofun Cluster]] [[ja:太秦古墳群]] [[sn:zumasa Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Gunshūfun]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]]

{{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Neyagawa|qid=Q389633}}; {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|qid=Q122723}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11444484]] Wikidata name: [[Category:Add wikidata]] [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Location map|Japan Osaka Prefecture|lat_deg=34|lat_min=45|lat_sec=28.60|lon_deg=135|lon_min=38|lon_sec=46.87|label=Uzumasa Kofun Group|position=top|width=220|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{center|Location of Uzumasa Kofun Group}}}} '''Uzumasa Kofun Group''' (太秦古墳群, Uzumasa Kofun-gun) refers to a cluster of ancient Kofuns (kofun) located on the eastern hills of [[:en:Neyagawa, Osaka|Neyagawa, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]]. Presently, all tumuli except the Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun (a Neyagawa City Designated Historic Site) have been lost. == Overview == [[File:Uzumasa Kofun Group-18, funkyu.jpg|thumb|220px|right|Restored Mound of Kofun No. 18]] [[File:太秦古墳群 出土品.JPG|thumb|220px|right|burial goods from the site
{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]].}]]] The Uzumasa Kofun Group once extended across the hills around Uzumasa Takatsuka Town and Kokumori Town. Besides the known Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun, fragments of [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] and [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] have been unearthed from various locations in the area. The presence of place names such as Koganezuka, Mukaihizuka, Mawashizuka, Morozuka, Kaminoyama, Hakunoyama, Matsutanidaniyama, and Dandaniyama suggests a broad distribution of Kofuns in the past. Notably, in 1962, a deer-shaped haniwa was excavated from behind the Uzumasa Atsuta Shrine. In 1965, 300 meters south of Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun, the remains of a burial believed to involve direct wooden coffin interment were discovered, yielding iron arrowheads and straight swords. Additional artifacts such as triple-ring bells, gold rings, bronze arrowheads, comma-shaped beads, and spindle whorls have also been found. Collectively, these findings are referred to as the Uzumasa Kofun GroupNishida (1994), p. 85.. From 2001 to 2003, the {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Cultural Properties Center|ja|大阪府文化財センター|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Prefectural Cultural Properties Center|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} conducted investigations in preparation for constructing the [[:en:Second Keihan Highway|Second Keihan Highway]], unearthing remains of 25 small Kofuns, mostly flattened, dating from the mid-5th to early 6th centuries. These mounds, located along a ridge, are referred to as the Uzumasa Kofun Group's Ridge Cluster. Additionally, Neyagawa City carried out excavations in 2001 for the preservation of Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun, fully uncovering its structure. === Ridge Cluster Investigations === Investigations for the [[:en:Second Keihan Highway|Second Keihan Highway]] revealed numerous remains of mound ditches along a ridge. Most mounds were already damaged, with burial structures identified in only one (Kofun No. 3), which contained a direct wooden coffin burial. These mounds ranged in size from 4 to 14 meters in diameter or length. Artifacts such as Sue pottery and [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] were recovered from the surrounding ditches, while haniwa were found at only two sites (Kofun Nos. 8 and 17), yielding cylindrical and lotus-shaped haniwa. Based on these finds, the Ridge Cluster is dated to the mid-5th to early 6th centuriesIchimoto (2005), p. 1.. === Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun === [[File:Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|thumb|Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun]] Located 800 meters northwest of the Ridge Cluster, Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun is the only mound within the group that remains intact. Excavations in 2001 revealed a two-tiered, 37-meter-diameter circular mound with a northwestern {{ill|Platform-style projection|lt=Platform-style projection|lt=Tsukuridashi|ja|造り出し|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukuridashi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 造出}} projection. The mound lacked stone coverings but had cylindrical haniwa rows on the terrace and Tsukuridashi. Figurative haniwa, including depictions of humans, houses, shields, roosters, and waterfowl, were also found. Artifacts such as armor, arrowheads, axes, and horse fittings were unearthed from the burial chamber at the mound's summit. These findings suggest the mound was constructed in the late 5th century. The significant differences in mound size and artifacts between Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun and the Ridge Cluster imply social stratification, with Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun representing a leader’s burial and the Ridge Cluster representing subordinate graves. Today, Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun is a Neyagawa City Designated Historic Site and is preserved within the Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun Park. File:Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Summit of the mound File:Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun, tsukuridashi-2.jpg|Tsukuridashi projection == Cultural Properties == === Neyagawa City Designated Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Properties ''' Deer-shaped Haniwa from Uzumasa Kofun Group (Archaeological Artifacts) - Stored at the Neyagawa City Buried Cultural Properties Museum. Designated on November 3, 2000[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html List of Designated Cultural Properties in Osaka Prefecture] (Osaka Prefecture website, updated November 21, 2018).. * Historic Sites ''' Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun - Designated on November 3, 1997. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Nishida Toshihide, "Kofun of Makimuku," in *Enjoyable Kofun Tour*, Shōraisha, 11. 84-86. * Ichimoto Yoshizo, "Uzumasa Kofun Group," in *Symposium: Understanding Osaka's Early Kofun*, Osaka Prefectural Yayoi Culture Museum, 11. 1-3, 2006. * *Uzumasa Kofun Group Excavation Results: Considering Osaka's Early Kofun Groups*, ed. Osaka Cultural Properties Center, 2006. == See Also == * {{ill|Nagahara Kofun Cluster|lt=Nagahara Kofun Cluster|lt=Nagahara Kofun Group|ja|長原古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagahara Kofun Group|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun|Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun}} * [https://www.city.neyagawa.osaka.jp/organization_list/kyoiku_shakaikyoiku/bunkasport/bunkazai/namesagasu/1378193397977.html Uzumasa Kofun Group], [https://www.city.neyagawa.osaka.jp/Recommend/bunkazai/neyagawashishiteibunkazai/1382432802559.html Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun] - Neyagawa City Official Website {{translated page|ja|太秦古墳群|version=81546838|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[sn:Uzumasa Kofun Cluster]] [[ja:太秦古墳群]] [[sn:zumasa Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Gunshūfun]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Uzumasa Kofun Cluster]][[Category:qq]]

From 2001 to 2003, the {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Cultural Properties Center|ja|大阪府文化財センター|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Prefectural Cultural Properties Center|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} conducted investigations in preparation for constructing the [[:en:Second Keihan Highway|Second Keihan Highway]], unearthing remains of 25 small Kofuns, mostly flattened, dating from the mid-5th to early 6th centuries. Additionally, Neyagawa City carried out excavations in 2001 for the preservation of Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun, fully uncovering its structure. === Ridge Cluster Investigations === Investigations for the [[:en:Second Keihan Highway|Second Keihan Highway]] revealed numerous remains of mound ditches along a ridge. Most mounds were already damaged, with burial structures identified in only one (Kofun No. 3), which contained a direct wooden coffin burial. These mounds ranged in size from 4 to 14 meters in diameter or length. Artifacts such as Sue pottery and [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] were recovered from the surrounding ditches, while haniwa were found at only two sites (Kofun Nos. 8 and 17), yielding cylindrical and lotus-shaped haniwa. Based on these finds, the Ridge Cluster is dated to the mid-5th to early 6th centuriesIchimoto (2005), p. 1.. === Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun === [[File:Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|thumb|Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun]] Located 800 meters northwest of the Ridge Cluster, Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun is the only mound within the group that remains intact. Excavations in 2001 revealed a two-tiered, 37-meter-diameter circular mound with a northwestern {{ill|Platform-style projection|lt=Platform-style projection|lt=Tsukuridashi|ja|造り出し|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukuridashi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 造出}} projection. The mound lacked stone coverings but had cylindrical haniwa rows on the terrace and Tsukuridashi. Figurative haniwa, including depictions of humans, houses, shields, roosters, and waterfowl, were also found. Artifacts such as armor, arrowheads, axes, and horse fittings were unearthed from the burial chamber at the mound's summit. These findings suggest the mound was constructed in the late 5th century. The significant differences in mound size and artifacts between Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun and the Ridge Cluster imply social stratification, with Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun representing a leader’s burial and the Ridge Cluster representing subordinate graves. Today, Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun is a Neyagawa City Designated Historic Site and is preserved within the Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun Park. File:Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Summit of the mound File:Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun, tsukuridashi-2.jpg|Tsukuridashi projection == Cultural Properties == === Neyagawa City Designated Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Properties ''' Deer-shaped Haniwa from Uzumasa Kofun Group (Archaeological Artifacts) - Stored at the Neyagawa City Buried Cultural Properties Museum. Designated on November 3, 2000[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html List of Designated Cultural Properties in Osaka Prefecture] (Osaka Prefecture website, updated November 21, 2018).. * Historic Sites ''' Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun - Designated on November 3, 1997. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Nishida Toshihide, "Kofun of Makimuku," in *Enjoyable Kofun Tour*, Shōraisha, 11. 84-86. * Ichimoto Yoshizo, "Uzumasa Kofun Group," in *Symposium: Understanding Osaka's Early Kofun*, Osaka Prefectural Yayoi Culture Museum, 11. 1-3, 2006. * *Uzumasa Kofun Group Excavation Results: Considering Osaka's Early Kofun Groups*, ed. Osaka Cultural Properties Center, 2006. == See Also == * {{ill|Nagahara Kofun Cluster|lt=Nagahara Kofun Cluster|lt=Nagahara Kofun Group|ja|長原古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagahara Kofun Group|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun|Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun}} * [https://www.city.neyagawa.osaka.jp/organization_list/kyoiku_shakaikyoiku/bunkasport/bunkazai/namesagasu/1378193397977.html Uzumasa Kofun Group], [https://www.city.neyagawa.osaka.jp/Recommend/bunkazai/neyagawashishiteibunkazai/1382432802559.html Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun] - Neyagawa City Official Website {{translated page|ja|太秦古墳群|version=81546838|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[sn:Uzumasa Kofun Cluster]] [[ja:太秦古墳群]] [[sn:zumasa Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Gunshūfun]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] [[en:Uzumasa Kofun Cluster]]

{{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Neyagawa|qid=Q389633}}; {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|qid=Q122723}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q11444484]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Location map|Japan Osaka Prefecture|lat_deg=34|lat_min=45|lat_sec=28.60|lon_deg=135|lon_min=38|lon_sec=46.87|label=Uzumasa Kofun Group|position=top|width=220|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{center|Location of Uzumasa Kofun Group}}}} '''Uzumasa Kofun Group''' (太秦古墳群, Uzumasa Kofun-gun) refers to a cluster of ancient Kofuns (kofun) located on the eastern hills of [[:en:Neyagawa, Osaka|Neyagawa, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]]. Presently, all tumuli except the Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun (a Neyagawa City Designated Historic Site) have been lost. == Overview == [[File:Uzumasa Kofun Group-18, funkyu.jpg|thumb|220px|right|Restored Mound of Kofun No. 18]] [[File:太秦古墳群 出土品.JPG|thumb|220px|right|burial goods from the site
{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]].}]]] The Uzumasa Kofun Group once extended across the hills around Uzumasa Takatsuka Town and Kokumori Town. Besides the known Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun, fragments of [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] and [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] have been unearthed from various locations in the area. The presence of place names such as Koganezuka, Mukaihizuka, Mawashizuka, Morozuka, Kaminoyama, Hakunoyama, Matsutanidaniyama, and Dandaniyama suggests a broad distribution of Kofuns in the past. Notably, in 1962, a deer-shaped haniwa was excavated from behind the Uzumasa Atsuta Shrine. In 1965, 300 meters south of Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun, the remains of a burial believed to involve direct wooden coffin interment were discovered, yielding iron arrowheads and straight swords. Additional artifacts such as triple-ring bells, gold rings, bronze arrowheads, comma-shaped beads, and spindle whorls have also been found. Collectively, these findings are referred to as the Uzumasa Kofun GroupNishida (1994), p. 85.. From 2001 to 2003, the {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Cultural Properties Center|ja|大阪府文化財センター|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Prefectural Cultural Properties Center|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} conducted investigations in preparation for constructing the [[:en:Second Keihan Highway|Second Keihan Highway]], unearthing remains of 25 small Kofuns, mostly flattened, dating from the mid-5th to early 6th centuries. These mounds, located along a ridge, are referred to as the Uzumasa Kofun Group's Ridge Cluster. Additionally, Neyagawa City carried out excavations in 2001 for the preservation of Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun, fully uncovering its structure. === Ridge Cluster Investigations === Investigations for the [[:en:Second Keihan Highway|Second Keihan Highway]] revealed numerous remains of mound ditches along a ridge. Most mounds were already damaged, with burial structures identified in only one (Kofun No. 3), which contained a direct wooden coffin burial. These mounds ranged in size from 4 to 14 meters in diameter or length. Artifacts such as Sue pottery and [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] were recovered from the surrounding ditches, while haniwa were found at only two sites (Kofun Nos. 8 and 17), yielding cylindrical and lotus-shaped haniwa. Based on these finds, the Ridge Cluster is dated to the mid-5th to early 6th centuriesIchimoto (2005), p. 1.. === Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun === [[File:Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|thumb|Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun]] Located 800 meters northwest of the Ridge Cluster, Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun is the only mound within the group that remains intact. Excavations in 2001 revealed a two-tiered, 37-meter-diameter circular mound with a northwestern {{ill|Platform-style projection|lt=Platform-style projection|lt=Tsukuridashi|ja|造り出し|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukuridashi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 造出}} projection. The mound lacked stone coverings but had cylindrical haniwa rows on the terrace and Tsukuridashi. Figurative haniwa, including depictions of humans, houses, shields, roosters, and waterfowl, were also found. Artifacts such as armor, arrowheads, axes, and horse fittings were unearthed from the burial chamber at the mound's summit. These findings suggest the mound was constructed in the late 5th century. The significant differences in mound size and artifacts between Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun and the Ridge Cluster imply social stratification, with Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun representing a leader’s burial and the Ridge Cluster representing subordinate graves. Today, Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun is a Neyagawa City Designated Historic Site and is preserved within the Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun Park. File:Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Summit of the mound File:Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun, tsukuridashi-2.jpg|Tsukuridashi projection == Cultural Properties == === Neyagawa City Designated Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Properties ''' Deer-shaped Haniwa from Uzumasa Kofun Group (Archaeological Artifacts) - Stored at the Neyagawa City Buried Cultural Properties Museum. Designated on November 3, 2000[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html List of Designated Cultural Properties in Osaka Prefecture] (Osaka Prefecture website, updated November 21, 2018).. * Historic Sites ''' Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun - Designated on November 3, 1997. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Nishida Toshihide, "Kofun of Makimuku," in *Enjoyable Kofun Tour*, Shōraisha, 11. 84-86. * Ichimoto Yoshizo, "Uzumasa Kofun Group," in *Symposium: Understanding Osaka's Early Kofun*, Osaka Prefectural Yayoi Culture Museum, 11. 1-3, 2006. * *Uzumasa Kofun Group Excavation Results: Considering Osaka's Early Kofun Groups*, ed. Osaka Cultural Properties Center, 2006. == See Also == * {{ill|Nagahara Kofun Cluster|lt=Nagahara Kofun Cluster|lt=Nagahara Kofun Group|ja|長原古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagahara Kofun Group|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun|Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun}} * [https://www.city.neyagawa.osaka.jp/organization_list/kyoiku_shakaikyoiku/bunkasport/bunkazai/namesagasu/1378193397977.html Uzumasa Kofun Group], [https://www.city.neyagawa.osaka.jp/Recommend/bunkazai/neyagawashishiteibunkazai/1382432802559.html Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun] - Neyagawa City Official Website {{translated page|ja|太秦古墳群|version=81546838|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[sn:Uzumasa Kofun Cluster]] [[ja:太秦古墳群]] [[sn:zumasa Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Gunshūfun]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] [[en:Uzumasa Kofun Cluster]] {{wikidata link|Q11444484}}

{{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Neyagawa|qid=Q389633}}; {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|qid=Q122723}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q11444484]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Location map|Japan Osaka Prefecture|lat_deg=34|lat_min=45|lat_sec=28.60|lon_deg=135|lon_min=38|lon_sec=46.87|label=Uzumasa Kofun Group|position=top|width=220|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{center|Location of Uzumasa Kofun Group}}}} '''Uzumasa Kofun Group''' (太秦古墳群, Uzumasa Kofun-gun) refers to a cluster of ancient Kofuns (kofun) located on the eastern hills of [[:en:Neyagawa, Osaka|Neyagawa, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]]. Presently, all tumuli except the Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun (a Neyagawa City Designated Historic Site) have been lost. == Overview == [[File:Uzumasa Kofun Group-18, funkyu.jpg|thumb|220px|right|Restored Mound of Kofun No. 18]] [[File:太秦古墳群 出土品.JPG|thumb|220px|right|burial goods from the site
{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]].}]]] The Uzumasa Kofun Group once extended across the hills around Uzumasa Takatsuka Town and Kokumori Town. Besides the known Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun, fragments of [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] and [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] have been unearthed from various locations in the area. The presence of place names such as Koganezuka, Mukaihizuka, Mawashizuka, Morozuka, Kaminoyama, Hakunoyama, Matsutanidaniyama, and Dandaniyama suggests a broad distribution of Kofuns in the past. Notably, in 1962, a deer-shaped haniwa was excavated from behind the Uzumasa Atsuta Shrine. In 1965, 300 meters south of Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun, the remains of a burial believed to involve direct wooden coffin interment were discovered, yielding iron arrowheads and straight swords. Additional artifacts such as triple-ring bells, gold rings, bronze arrowheads, comma-shaped beads, and spindle whorls have also been found. Collectively, these findings are referred to as the Uzumasa Kofun GroupNishida (1994), p. 85.. From 2001 to 2003, the {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Cultural Properties Center|ja|大阪府文化財センター|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Prefectural Cultural Properties Center|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} conducted investigations in preparation for constructing the [[:en:Second Keihan Highway|Second Keihan Highway]], unearthing remains of 25 small Kofuns, mostly flattened, dating from the mid-5th to early 6th centuries. These mounds, located along a ridge, are referred to as the Uzumasa Kofun Group's Ridge Cluster. Additionally, Neyagawa City carried out excavations in 2001 for the preservation of Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun, fully uncovering its structure. === Ridge Cluster Investigations === Investigations for the [[:en:Second Keihan Highway|Second Keihan Highway]] revealed numerous remains of mound ditches along a ridge. Most mounds were already damaged, with burial structures identified in only one (Kofun No. 3), which contained a direct wooden coffin burial. These mounds ranged in size from 4 to 14 meters in diameter or length. Artifacts such as Sue pottery and [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] were recovered from the surrounding ditches, while haniwa were found at only two sites (Kofun Nos. 8 and 17), yielding cylindrical and lotus-shaped haniwa. Based on these finds, the Ridge Cluster is dated to the mid-5th to early 6th centuriesIchimoto (2005), p. 1.. === Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun === [[File:Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|thumb|Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun]] Located 800 meters northwest of the Ridge Cluster, Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun is the only mound within the group that remains intact. Excavations in 2001 revealed a two-tiered, 37-meter-diameter circular mound with a northwestern {{ill|Platform-style projection|lt=Platform-style projection|lt=Tsukuridashi|ja|造り出し|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukuridashi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 造出}} projection. The mound lacked stone coverings but had cylindrical haniwa rows on the terrace and Tsukuridashi. Figurative haniwa, including depictions of humans, houses, shields, roosters, and waterfowl, were also found. Artifacts such as armor, arrowheads, axes, and horse fittings were unearthed from the burial chamber at the mound's summit. These findings suggest the mound was constructed in the late 5th century. The significant differences in mound size and artifacts between Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun and the Ridge Cluster imply social stratification, with Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun representing a leader’s burial and the Ridge Cluster representing subordinate graves. Today, Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun is a Neyagawa City Designated Historic Site and is preserved within the Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun Park. File:Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Summit of the mound File:Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun, tsukuridashi-2.jpg|Tsukuridashi projection == Cultural Properties == === Neyagawa City Designated Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Properties ''' Deer-shaped Haniwa from Uzumasa Kofun Group (Archaeological Artifacts) - Stored at the Neyagawa City Buried Cultural Properties Museum. Designated on November 3, 2000[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html List of Designated Cultural Properties in Osaka Prefecture] (Osaka Prefecture website, updated November 21, 2018).. * Historic Sites ''' Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun - Designated on November 3, 1997. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Nishida Toshihide, "Kofun of Makimuku," in *Enjoyable Kofun Tour*, Shōraisha, 11. 84-86. * Ichimoto Yoshizo, "Uzumasa Kofun Group," in *Symposium: Understanding Osaka's Early Kofun*, Osaka Prefectural Yayoi Culture Museum, 11. 1-3, 2006. * *Uzumasa Kofun Group Excavation Results: Considering Osaka's Early Kofun Groups*, ed. Osaka Cultural Properties Center, 2006. == See Also == * {{ill|Nagahara Kofun Cluster|lt=Nagahara Kofun Cluster|lt=Nagahara Kofun Group|ja|長原古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagahara Kofun Group|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun|Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun}} * [https://www.city.neyagawa.osaka.jp/organization_list/kyoiku_shakaikyoiku/bunkasport/bunkazai/namesagasu/1378193397977.html Uzumasa Kofun Group], [https://www.city.neyagawa.osaka.jp/Recommend/bunkazai/neyagawashishiteibunkazai/1382432802559.html Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun] - Neyagawa City Official Website {{translated page|ja|太秦古墳群|version=81546838|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:太秦古墳群]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Gunshūfun]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] [[Category:Qq 4]] {{wikidata link|Q11444484}}

Artifacts such as Sue pottery and [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] were recovered from the surrounding ditches, while haniwa were found at only two sites (Kofun Nos. 8 and 17), yielding cylindrical and lotus-shaped haniwa. Based on these finds, the Ridge Cluster is dated to the mid-5th to early 6th centuriesIchimoto (2005), p. 1.. === Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun === [[File:Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|thumb|Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun]] Located 800 meters northwest of the Ridge Cluster, Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun is the only mound within the group that remains intact. Excavations in 2001 revealed a two-tiered, 37-meter-diameter circular mound with a northwestern {{ill|Platform-style projection|lt=Platform-style projection|lt=Tsukuridashi|ja|造り出し|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukuridashi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 造出}} projection. The mound lacked stone coverings but had cylindrical haniwa rows on the terrace and Tsukuridashi. Figurative haniwa, including depictions of humans, houses, shields, roosters, and waterfowl, were also found. Artifacts such as armor, arrowheads, axes, and horse fittings were unearthed from the burial chamber at the mound's summit. These findings suggest the mound was constructed in the late 5th century. The significant differences in mound size and artifacts between Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun and the Ridge Cluster imply social stratification, with Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun representing a leader’s burial and the Ridge Cluster representing subordinate graves. Today, Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun is a Neyagawa City Designated Historic Site and is preserved within the Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun Park. File:Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Summit of the mound File:Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun, tsukuridashi-2.jpg|Tsukuridashi projection == Cultural Properties == === Neyagawa City Designated Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Properties ''' Deer-shaped Haniwa from Uzumasa Kofun Group (Archaeological Artifacts) - Stored at the Neyagawa City Buried Cultural Properties Museum. Designated on November 3, 2000[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html List of Designated Cultural Properties in Osaka Prefecture] (Osaka Prefecture website, updated November 21, 2018).. * Historic Sites ''' Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun - Designated on November 3, 1997. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Nishida Toshihide, "Kofun of Makimuku," in *Enjoyable Kofun Tour*, Shōraisha, 11. 84-86. * Ichimoto Yoshizo, "Uzumasa Kofun Group," in *Symposium: Understanding Osaka's Early Kofun*, Osaka Prefectural Yayoi Culture Museum, 11. 1-3, 2006. * *Uzumasa Kofun Group Excavation Results: Considering Osaka's Early Kofun Groups*, ed. [[ja:太秦古墳群]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Neyagawa|WD=Q389633}}; {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q11444484]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Location map|Japan Osaka Prefecture|lat_deg=34|lat_min=45|lat_sec=28.60|lon_deg=135|lon_min=38|lon_sec=46.87|label=Uzumasa Kofun Group|position=top|width=220|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{center|Location of Uzumasa Kofun Group}}}} '''Uzumasa Kofun Group''' (太秦古墳群, Uzumasa Kofun-gun) refers to a cluster of ancient Kofuns (kofun) located on the eastern hills of [[:en:Neyagawa, Osaka|Neyagawa, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]]. Presently, all tumuli except the Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun (a Neyagawa City Designated Historic Site) have been lost. == Overview == [[File:Uzumasa Kofun Group-18, funkyu.jpg|thumb|220px|right|Restored Mound of Kofun No. 18]] [[File:太秦古墳群 出土品.JPG|thumb|220px|right|burial goods from the site
{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Chikatsu Asuka Museum]].}]]] The Uzumasa Kofun Group once extended across the hills around Uzumasa Takatsuka Town and Kokumori Town. Besides the known Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun, fragments of [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] and [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] have been unearthed from various locations in the area. The presence of place names such as Koganezuka, Mukaihizuka, Mawashizuka, Morozuka, Kaminoyama, Hakunoyama, Matsutanidaniyama, and Dandaniyama suggests a broad distribution of Kofuns in the past. Notably, in 1962, a deer-shaped haniwa was excavated from behind the Uzumasa Atsuta Shrine. In 1965, 300 meters south of Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun, the remains of a burial believed to involve direct wooden coffin interment were discovered, yielding iron arrowheads and straight swords. Additional artifacts such as triple-ring bells, gold rings, bronze arrowheads, comma-shaped beads, and spindle whorls have also been found. Collectively, these findings are referred to as the Uzumasa Kofun GroupNishida (1994), p. 85.. From 2001 to 2003, the {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Cultural Properties Center|ja|大阪府文化財センター|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Prefectural Cultural Properties Center|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} conducted investigations in preparation for constructing the [[:en:Second Keihan Highway|Second Keihan Highway]], unearthing remains of 25 small Kofuns, mostly flattened, dating from the mid-5th to early 6th centuries. These mounds, located along a ridge, are referred to as the Uzumasa Kofun Group's Ridge Cluster. Additionally, Neyagawa City carried out excavations in 2001 for the preservation of Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun, fully uncovering its structure. === Ridge Cluster Investigations === Investigations for the [[:en:Second Keihan Highway|Second Keihan Highway]] revealed numerous remains of mound ditches along a ridge. Most mounds were already damaged, with burial structures identified in only one (Kofun No. 3), which contained a direct wooden coffin burial. These mounds ranged in size from 4 to 14 meters in diameter or length. Artifacts such as Sue pottery and [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] were recovered from the surrounding ditches, while haniwa were found at only two sites (Kofun Nos. 8 and 17), yielding cylindrical and lotus-shaped haniwa. Based on these finds, the Ridge Cluster is dated to the mid-5th to early 6th centuriesIchimoto (2005), p. 1.. === Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun === [[File:Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|thumb|Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun]] Located 800 meters northwest of the Ridge Cluster, Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun is the only mound within the group that remains intact. Excavations in 2001 revealed a two-tiered, 37-meter-diameter circular mound with a northwestern {{ill|Platform-style projection|lt=Platform-style projection|lt=Tsukuridashi|ja|造り出し|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukuridashi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 造出}} projection. The mound lacked stone coverings but had cylindrical haniwa rows on the terrace and Tsukuridashi. Figurative haniwa, including depictions of humans, houses, shields, roosters, and waterfowl, were also found. Artifacts such as armor, arrowheads, axes, and horse fittings were unearthed from the burial chamber at the mound's summit. These findings suggest the mound was constructed in the late 5th century. The significant differences in mound size and artifacts between Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun and the Ridge Cluster imply social stratification, with Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun representing a leader’s burial and the Ridge Cluster representing subordinate graves. Today, Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun is a Neyagawa City Designated Historic Site and is preserved within the Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun Park. File:Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Summit of the mound File:Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun, tsukuridashi-2.jpg|Tsukuridashi projection == Cultural Properties == === Neyagawa City Designated Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Properties ''' Deer-shaped Haniwa from Uzumasa Kofun Group (Archaeological Artifacts) - Stored at the Neyagawa City Buried Cultural Properties Museum. Designated on November 3, 2000[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html List of Designated Cultural Properties in Osaka Prefecture] (Osaka Prefecture website, updated November 21, 2018).. * Historic Sites ''' Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun - Designated on November 3, 1997. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Nishida Toshihide, "Kofun of Makimuku," in *Enjoyable Kofun Tour*, Shōraisha, 11. 84-86. * Ichimoto Yoshizo, "Uzumasa Kofun Group," in *Symposium: Understanding Osaka's Early Kofun*, Osaka Prefectural Yayoi Culture Museum, 11. 1-3, 2006. * *Uzumasa Kofun Group Excavation Results: Considering Osaka's Early Kofun Groups*, ed. Osaka Cultural Properties Center, 2006. == See Also == * {{ill|Nagahara Kofun Cluster|lt=Nagahara Kofun Cluster|lt=Nagahara Kofun Group|ja|長原古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagahara Kofun Group|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun|Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun}} * [https://www.city.neyagawa.osaka.jp/organization_list/kyoiku_shakaikyoiku/bunkasport/bunkazai/namesagasu/1378193397977.html Uzumasa Kofun Group], [https://www.city.neyagawa.osaka.jp/Recommend/bunkazai/neyagawashishiteibunkazai/1382432802559.html Uzumasa Takatsuka Kofun] - Neyagawa City Official Website {{translated page|ja|太秦古墳群|version=81546838|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:太秦古墳群]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Gunshūfun]] {{wikidata link|Q11444484}}
